issue_title: In pants goal idea, some users are seeing ivy failures trying to download a -sources jar that does not exist.
issue_body:
This issue started with a discussion in https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/pants-devel/xfrYj0_UB-c

Square contributed a change to the ivy_resolve and ide_gen steps that pulls in 'sources' and 'javadoc' confs automatically when the user tries `./pants idea <target>`

When this feature was initially proposed, there was a concern noted in  https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/716/ that sometimes sources or javadocs might not be available and downloads would fail.  We wrote an [integration test](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/aca4aab4e081ffb0b9d1895e9922f359bb42da1f/tests/python/pants_test/tasks/test_idea_integration.py#L211) to show that Ivy would ignore missing sources or javadoc downloads.  

Usually, this feature works for us, but some users have seen a ivy get 'stuck' trying to download a missing sources javadoc.    Here is the ivy output we were seeing from the problem:

```
...
org.apache.httpcomponents#httpclient;4.1.2 by[org.apache.httpcomponents#httpclient;4.3.6](https://jira.corp.squareup.com/browse/STWO-169?jql=project%20%3D%20STWO%20AND%20resolution%20%3D%20Unresolved%20AND%20component%20%3D%20%22Pants%20%2F%20Java%20Build%22%20ORDER%20BY%20priority%20DESC#httpclient;4.3.6) in [default]
---------------------------------------------------------------------

 modules
artifacts
confnumbersearchdwnldedevicted
numberdwnlded

---------------------------------------------------------------------

default1770022
1550
sources105000
1020
javadoc105000
970

---------------------------------------------------------------------
:: problems summary ::
:::: WARNINGS
[NOT FOUND ] log4j#apache-log4j-extras;1.2.17!apache-log4j-extras.jar(source) (564ms)
==== remote-repos-square-maven: tried
[https://nexus.corp.squareup.com/content/groups/public/log4j/apache-log4j-extras/1.2.17/apache-log4j-extras-1.2.17-sources.jar](https://nexus.corp.squareup.com/content/groups/public/log4j/apache-log4j-extras/1.2.17/apache-log4j-extras-1.2.17-sources.jar)
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:: FAILED DOWNLOADS ::
:: ^ see resolution messages for details ^ ::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:: log4j#apache-log4j-extras;1.2.17!apache-log4j-extras.jar(source)
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
```

Then Ivy returns non-zero to the shell causing pants to exit.

To work around this problem, I submitted  https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/1939/ so that these users could run goal idea without requesting javadoc and sources.  This works, but we would like something more elegant.

I added more debugging and saw that the user's install is actually going out to our nexus and getting a 404 returned for this jar file.  I tried to reproduce using the same ivy.xml and same ivysettings.xml from inside of square and my installation didn't have any problems.  Then I moved the ivy cache out of the way and still didn't have any problems starting over from an empty cache.  The user reported moving ~/.ivy2 out of the way and still running into the issue.

```console

...
Successfully installed Markdown Pygments ansicolors cov-core coverage docutils lockfile pantsbuild.pants pex psutil py pystache pytest pytest-cov requests six twitter.common.collections twitter.common.config twitter.common.confluence twitter.common.contextutil twitter.common.decorators twitter.common.dirutil twitter.common.lang twitter.common.log twitter.common.options twitter.common.process twitter.common.string twitter.common.threading twitter.common.util setuptools

  File "/home/vagrant/apache-aurora-0.7.0-incubating/build-support/pants.venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pants/bin/pants_exe.py", line 66, in main

  File "/home/vagrant/apache-aurora-0.7.0-incubating/build-support/pants.venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pants/bin/pants_exe.py", line 60, in _run
    goal_runner.setup()
  File "/home/vagrant/apache-aurora-0.7.0-incubating/build-support/pants.venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pants/bin/goal_runner.py", line 104, in setup
    self.run_tracker.run_info.add_scm_info()
  File "/home/vagrant/apache-aurora-0.7.0-incubating/build-support/pants.venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pants/base/run_info.py", line 86, in add_scm_info
    scm = get_scm()
  File "/home/vagrant/apache-aurora-0.7.0-incubating/build-support/pants.venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pants/base/build_environment.py", line 48, in get_scm
    worktree = Git.detect_worktree()
  File "/home/vagrant/apache-aurora-0.7.0-incubating/build-support/pants.venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pants/scm/git.py", line 22, in detect_worktree
    process, out = cls._invoke(cmd)
  File "/home/vagrant/apache-aurora-0.7.0-incubating/build-support/pants.venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pants/scm/git.py", line 36, in _invoke
    process = subprocess.Popen(cmd, stdout=subprocess.PIPE)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/subprocess.py", line 679, in __init__
    errread, errwrite)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/subprocess.py", line 1249, in _execute_child
    raise child_exception

Exception message: [Errno 2] No such file or directory
```

This should have been done earlier, but glibc headers were available on all Linux systems tested in standard-enough places for clang to find. However, gcc does have issues finding these headers in Travis (see [this FIXME](https://github.com/cosmicexplorer/pants/blob/c35ac057694af4b3c410efec681a7b60592ea9cb/tests/python/pants_test/backend/native/subsystems/test_native_toolchain.py#L17) from #5490) -- while that FIXME says to use clang's driver to find include directories for either clang or gcc, we should probably instead just provide the entire toolchain.

Reasoning: Trying to invoke `gcc` will fail on travis, but `clang` will work, because the clang we package is able to find object files and headers that gcc cannot: see [this CI failure](https://travis-ci.org/pantsbuild/pants/jobs/361959908), I was able to "fix" that by debugging on an `ubuntu:trusty` image, but found other errors: specifically, clang's preprocessor could find `/usr/include/x86_64-linux-gnu`, while gcc was hopelessly stumbling around for `/usr/include/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu`.

This would need #5661 to affect `python_dist()` targets (otherwise it is difficult to perform this sort of configuration).

issue_title: Store and populate DirectoryDigest for cached zinc compile vts
issue_body:
https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/pull/6351 adds `DirectoryDigest` capture for hermetic executions of zinc compiles. However, nothing populates these `DirectoryDigest`s for cache hits; so cache hits won't have any files present on the classpath when compiling.

We should:
1. Modify `CompileContext` so that `classes_dir` and `jar_file` are of type `ClasspathEntry` rather than string. Modify all callers appropriately. (Possibly via making `ClasspathEntry` a `datatype`). This can initially be done by setting the value of the value of the `ClasspathEntry`'s `DirectoryDigest` to `None`.
2. Modify the zinc compile code so that when the `execute_process_synchronously` call finishes, we write a file named `directory_digest` or similar in the target working directory which contains some representation of the `DirectoryDigest`.
3. Modify the cache hit case to read this file, and populate the `DirectoryDigest` from the file (adding it as the third value to the tuple here): https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/432f03023af0bffe9f21fb475727c441a591e544/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/jvm_compile/jvm_compile.py#L420-L426

I'm imagining that `ClasspathEntry` will probably want to lazy-load and cache the digest from the file to centralise the logic, but maybe it should just live in `JvmCompile.do_compile`.

issue_title: Move rule declarations into decorators on functions

issue_body:
Currently CI is manually sharded: 2 unit test shards, 7 integration test shards and 2 contrib shards. [TestProjectsIntegrationTest](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/tests/python/pants_test/projects/test_testprojects_integration.py#L15) was modified a while back to spread tests over shards more evenly and with less maintenance. Consider generalizing this slightly with the end goal of just 4 CI buckets:
1. linux native engine build
2. osx native engine build
3. self checks
4. all tests

The all tests shards would then run a mix of unit, integration and contrib tests evenly spread over a pool of 7 shards.

issue_title: Add support to the `repl` goal for Node.js
issue_body: All the hard work is already done by `resolve.npm`, `repl.node` should will just need to consume the [`NpmResolve.NodePaths`](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/contrib/node/src/python/pants/contrib/node/tasks/npm_resolve.py#L50) product to launch node with an appropriate NODE_PATH.

While #4349 improves performance, cycle detection is effectively lost due to the new `SelectTransitive` does not keep track of links, we could either add the cycle detection back within `ST` that would require reconstructing the graph and redo the work already implemented in `Graph`, or figure out sending the link info to `Graph` and reuse the existing cycle detection.

We have been using wal-e for a while now and it has been working great. Occasionally, we seen issues where the `wal-push` command hangs forever. It has started occurring more frequently as of recent. I've tried to debug it and it looks like its stuck at doing something with gevent: I'm assuming this might be because of a network blip or the like.

I'm confused by the amount of disk space that my wal-e backups are taking up (and the associated AWS bill).

The total size of all my databases is a smidgen over 3GB.
Yet after running `wal-e` on an empty bucket for 12 days the total disk usage of that folder is`161GB`.

I run a full `backup-push` every week.
Also, I run a weekly job to delete all but the last five backups.

    /usr/local/bin/wal-e --terse delete --confirm retain 5

A few weeks back my S3 bucket had grown to half a terrabyte.
I ran the command to delete everything but after it had completed I still had a folder with `345GB` of data.
I cleaned that up by hand to start again from scratch.

When I now run the command to show backup size I see that I have three backups each around `4GB` in size.
Yet the total usage is `161GB` and rising.

Here are the relevant settings from my `postgresql.conf`.
```
wal_buffers = 16MB
wal_level = archive
archive_mode = on
archive_command = 'wal-e --terse wal-push %p'
archive_timeout = 60
```

Is all this normal (and if so, why) or am I doing something wrong?
Thanks, Darren.

I wanted to add custom tab positions at fixed positions for particular paragraphs or styles and did not find an easy way to do this in docx. A quick scroll through the issues list found some example code which I have adapted. If anyone wants to tidy this up and add this to this fantastic library...

To use:

```python
        from docx import Document
        from docx.shared import Inches, Cm

        document = Document()

        # add to style
        style = document.document.styles["Header 2"]
        add_custom_tabs_to_paragraph_format(style.paragraph_format, [Cm(tt) for tt in range(1, 5)])

        # add to paragraph
        pp = document.add_paragraph("test", 3)
        add_custom_tabs_to_paragraph_format(pp.paragraph_format, [Cm(tt) for tt in range(1, 10)])
        document.save("test.docx")
```

Custom Tab Code:
```python

#__________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

#   add_custom_tabs_to_paragraph_format

#__________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
def add_custom_tabs_to_paragraph_format(paragraph_format, tab_stops, tab_style = "left", tab_leader = None):
    """
    tab_stops specified as list of Cm or Inches
    N.B. Hanging indents create an implicit tab stop

        <w:pPr>
              <w:tabs><w:tab w:val="left" w:pos="567"/>
                  <w:tab w:val="left" w:pos="1134"/>
                  <w:tab w:val="left" w:pos="1701"/>
                  <w:tab w:val="left" w:pos="2268"/></w:tabs>
            ...
        </w:pPr>

    """
    if tab_style not in ["left", "right", "center", "decimal", "bar", "clear", "end", "num", "start", ]:
        raise Exception("tab_style = %s is not a valid tab style in add_custom_tabs_to_paragraph_format(...)" % tab_style )
    if tab_leader not in [None, "dot", "heavy", "hyphen", "middleDot", "none", "underscore", ]:
        raise Exception("tab_leader = %s is not a valid tab leader in add_custom_tabs_to_paragraph_format(...)" % tab_leader )


    from docx.oxml import OxmlElement
    from docx.oxml.ns import qn

    def first_child_found_in(parent, tagnames):
        """
        Return the first child of parent with tag in *tagnames*, or None if
        not found.
        """
        for tagname in tagnames:
            child = parent.find(qn(tagname))
            if child is not None:
                return child
        return None

    def insert_element_before(parent, elm, successors):
        """
        Insert *elm* as child of *parent* before any existing child having
        tag name found in *successors*.
        """
        successor = first_child_found_in(parent, successors)
        if successor is not None:
            successor.addprevious(elm)
        else:
            parent.append(elm)
        return elm


    elem = paragraph_format.element  # elem is the <w:style> XML element
    pPr = elem.get_or_add_pPr()
    pTabs = OxmlElement('w:tabs')
    insert_element_before(pPr, pTabs, successors=(
        'w:shd', 'w:tabs', 'w:suppressAutoHyphens', 'w:kinsoku', 'w:wordWrap',
        'w:overflowPunct', 'w:topLinePunct', 'w:autoSpaceDE', 'w:autoSpaceDN',
        'w:bidi', 'w:adjustRightInd', 'w:snapToGrid', 'w:spacing', 'w:ind',
        'w:contextualSpacing', 'w:mirrorIndents', 'w:suppressOverlap', 'w:jc',
        'w:textDirection', 'w:textAlignment', 'w:textboxTightWrap',
        'w:outlineLvl', 'w:divId', 'w:cnfStyle', 'w:rPr', 'w:sectPr',
        'w:pPrChange'
    ))

    for tt in tab_stops:
        tab_n = OxmlElement('w:tab')
        tab_n.set(qn('w:val'), tab_style)
        tab_n.set(qn('w:pos'), '%s' % (tt / 625))
        if tab_leader is not None:
            tab_n.set(qn('w:leader'), tab_leader)
        pTabs.append(tab_n)

```

I will be using this for a personal project using async. As it stands currently, it does not support async at all. I managed a hack to get it to work for the time being, but it is sort of ugly using a hacky check and whatnot.

I would like to add proper async support, using with https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp . But as this will be a big change, I want to see up front whether or not this kind of change (PR) would be accepted here, or if I should make my own library separate.

The reason this would require a lot of changes is because the I/O is coupled to every class that makes a call to the plaid API. To make it properly support both synchronous and async methods, the I/O needs to be removed and passed in as a parameter (though, defaulting the the default non async class). So a handful of changes need to be done. 

I would like to contribute back and make this available here for every one to use. But I want to make sure it is something that will be accepted. Due to the nature of the change. I am fine maintaining my own version, mind you. I just thought I would try here directly first and to get your thoughts.

Automatic sign out form learner's Kolibri account when browser is closed or when tablet is powered off or after some stipulated time of no activity.

- The kids have the tendency to forget to sign out of their account:

 - Operating system: Ubuntu 14.04LTS
 - Browser: Chrome 

## How to reproduce

1. Log in as a learner.
2. Power of the tablets.
3. Switch on and Access the Kolibri page.
4. You will be logged into previously logged learner account.

issue_title: detect and warn of misconfigured server
issue_body:
Admins occasionally run into issues where people can't interact with streaming content and spend time trying to debug the cause. This can occur because either the user is running the devserver, or because of misconfigured nginx front-ends.

For example:
* https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/issues/676
* https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/issues/1165
* https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/issues/1655

If possible, it would be great to detect a misconfigured server and show a warning to the user.

issue_title: CLI: Initialization is run too early for some commands
issue_body:
### Observed behavior

We should exempt some commands like `dbrestore` and `help` from creating a database. Maybe also some other commands.

Since https://github.com/learningequality/morango/issues/48 was fixed, we should AFAIK be able to load the Django stack without any database connection. Anything else would be a regression.

Running `kolibri manage help` will bootstrap the database.

Related:

#2137

### Expected behavior

User can invoke CLI on a blank installation or during troubleshooting without this overhead. This may also involve `kolibri status` and `kolibri stop`.

### User-facing consequences

Creating a database and bootstrapping a `~/.kolibri` at the wrong time.

### Errors and logs

n/a

### Steps to reproduce

Run `kolibri manage help` on a blank installation.

### Context

All versions released

Current unstyled links:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2367265/23178443/478f9cfa-f81f-11e6-91ef-ae5c0f2e0842.png)


Intended design: 

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2367265/23178496/7c3f2be6-f81f-11e6-9a34-b6c5d51b749c.png)

(see https://projects.invisionapp.com/share/S7A3FGVW9#/screens/215056232)

### Observed behavior

https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/commit/d3582ba6dc5c1c76ae1aa28b431d34de6c7eadfc#r28674353

### Expected behavior

The language should specify Brazilian Portuguese

### User-facing consequences

slight language confusion

issue_title: Database is accessed during app config
issue_body:
Morango is currently hitting the database during `django.setup()` which is a problem for the database consistency check and database recovery (will never be able to use the Kolibri `dbrestore` command).

issue_title: Error due to comparison between int and str
issue_body: As reported in https://github.com/nccgroup/Scout2/issues/180

I've got an HTML document that contains some text inside of a table row: ```html foo
``` It looks like the text gets pulled outside of the table element for some reason (not sure how to print it with normal HTML-style syntax, but you can still see that `foo` has been moved): ```python >>> import html5lib >>> import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET >>> ET.tostring(html5lib.parse('foo
')) b'foo' ``` This is despite the example parsing correctly when using just `xml.etree`: ```python >>> import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET >>> ET.tostring(ET.ElementTree(ET.fromstring('foo
')).getroot()) b'foo

issue_title: `charm build` should be connecting to interfaces.juju.solutions with TLS
issue_body:
The connection to interfaces.juju.solutions should be TLS-secured, since it is used to redirect `charm build` to authoritative Git repositories for each layer/interface registered. An attacker could replace legitimate layer and interface repos with their own, and inject malicious code into a charm at build time.

I recommend [Let's Encrypt](https://letsencrypt.org/getting-started/) if you're managing this site yourselves. It can be easily set up in minutes.

## Checklist

 - [X] Confirmed this is an issue with charm-tools, not charmstore-client
 - [x] Provide versions of tools used
 - [x] Described the feature or ways to replicate the issue

## What version am I running?

I ran the following command: `charm version` and got the following ouput:

```
$ charm version
charm 2.1.1-0ubuntu1
charm-tools 2.1.2
```

I am using: (Your operating system)

```
$ cat /etc/lsb-release 
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=16.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=xenial
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 16.04 LTS"
```

## Issue/Feature

### I expect/expected the following

Connections to `https://interfaces.juju.solutions`. Automatic redirect from `http://interfaces.juju.solutions` to `https://interfaces.juju.solutions` when loading the page in a browser. https:// in URLs when grepping the charmtools sources.

### What I got

No redirect. Insecure connection to `http://interfaces.juju.solutions`.

issue_title: Make it possible for BLAST results to be processed using an sqlite FASTA database
issue_body: When processing DIAMOND results we can build a FASTA sqlite database to access subject sequences. With BLAST we get the subjects via `blastdbcmd` but that is sometimes unreliable so it would be good to also have the option of using an sqlite database or just providing a FASTA file so an in-memory `dict` can be built (also an option with DIAMOND). If neither of those options are given the BLAST code can fall back to using `blastdbcmd`.

issue_title: JRE 9 does not expose java.corba by default
issue_body:
I ran into this error when trying to run on Windows with JRE 9 installed:

```
Using SAS Config named: winiomwin
Java Error:
Error: Unable to initialize main class pyiom.saspy2j
Caused by: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: org/omg/CORBA/UserException
```

The [dependency here](https://github.com/sassoftware/saspy/blob/c6f57f72185cf0826a68180b76b7e117f8c070ad/saspy/java/pyiom/saspy2j.java#L8) is the culprit.  This module is [not available by default as of JRE 9](https://www.reddit.com/r/java/comments/4mzz7w/java_9_will_remove_corba_from_default_classpath/).

Resolved by adding the module explicitly in my `sascfg_personal.py`, e.g. for me this looks like:

```py
winiomwin = {
  "java": "java",
  "encoding": "windows-1252",
  "classpath": cpW,
  "javaparms": ["--add-modules=java.corba"],
}
```

issue_title: Invalid symlinks now break build, previously were skipped, skipped by docker CLI
issue_body:
Version 2.7.0 seems to die if you try to include a symlink that points to something that doesn't exist.  This problem seems to have been introduced in this commit:
 https://github.com/docker/docker-py/commit/5c5705045be72530091a51372ae920f958192bfb

Example from 2.6.1 and `docker` cli tool:
```
$ mkdir test
$ cd test
$ ln -s /doesnt_exist doesnt_exist
$ cat << EOF > Dockerfile 
FROM scratch

COPY . /tmp
EOF
$ docker build -t test . 
Sending build context to Docker daemon   2.56kB
Step 1/2 : FROM scratch
 ---> 
Step 2/2 : COPY . /tmp
 ---> cb6f16dc4b1f
Removing intermediate container 4eda4dd7d7c4
Successfully built cb6f16dc4b1f
Successfully tagged test:latest
$ python
Python 2.7.6 (default, Nov 23 2017, 15:49:48) 
[GCC 4.8.4] on linux2
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import docker
>>> docker.__version__
'2.6.1'
>>> docker.utils.create_archive(".", ['doesnt_exist'])
<open file '<fdopen>', mode 'w+b' at 0x7fa47208f8a0>
>>> 
```

But as of 2.7.0:
```
$ python
Python 2.7.6 (default, Nov 23 2017, 15:49:48) 
[GCC 4.8.4] on linux2
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import docker
>>> docker.__version__
'2.7.0'

    'Can not access file in context: {}'.format(full_path)
IOError: Can not access file in context: ./doesnt_exist

It seems that symlinks should be skipped rather than trying to checked to see if they are accessible, as was the previous behavior and the `docker` CLI tool behavior.

issue_title: Weight axis should use weightClass property
issue_body: According to section “Variation Axis Tags” in the [OpenType specification for “fvar”](https://www.microsoft.com/typography/otspec/fvar.htm), the values for the weight axis must correspond to [usWeightClass in “OS/2”](https://www.microsoft.com/typography/otspec/os2.htm#wtc). For example, Light must be at `wght=300`, Regular at `wght=400`, Black at `wght=900`. Currently, however, fontmake produces variable fonts whose weight axis is violating the OpenType spec: ``` xml wght 26.0 90.0 190.0 ``` Our Glyphs sources already contain the needed information as a `weightClass` property: But fontmake currently seems to ignore this property. Instead, it should use the property so that the produced variable font has the correct (according to spec) range for weight. Also, the produced font should not contain an `xxxx` axis; see https://github.com/googlei18n/fontmake/issues/144.

issue_body: There are some enums that are for External Calibration. We do not support Ext Cal in Python so we should remove those enums.

issue_title: Documentation should encourage restricing install to compatible versions
issue_body:
This project is following semver.  The documentation should encourage the user to follow best practices, including helping them from accidentally upgrade to an incompatible version until they are ready

For example, instead of 
```bash
$ python -m pip install nidaqmx
```
have
```bash
$ python -m pip install nidaqmx~=0.5.0
```

According to [PEP 440](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0440/#compatible-release), the compatible release version specifier will translate from `~=0.5.0` to `>=0.5.0,==0.5.*`

pip install  forex-python
Collecting forex-python
  Using cached forex-python-0.3.2.tar.gz
    Complete output from command python setup.py egg_info:
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
      File "/private/var/folders/h1/nbbcsvp10jj11nqx1kf0j_6h0000gn/T/pip-build-jwkn3_qf/forex-python/setup.py", line 14, in <module>
        long_description="\n\n".join([open("README.rst").read()]),
      File "/Users/razvan/dev/venv/bin/../lib/python3.5/encodings/ascii.py", line 26, in decode
        return codecs.ascii_decode(input, self.errors)[0]
    UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xc2 in position 3796: ordinal not in range(128)

    ----------------------------------------
Command "python setup.py egg_info" failed with error code 1 in /private/var/folders/h1/nbbcsvp10jj11nqx1kf0j_6h0000gn/T/pip-build-jwkn3_qf/forex-python/

RFC 6455 says:

> The masking key is a 32-bit value chosen at random by the client.
> When preparing a masked frame, the client MUST pick a fresh masking
> key from the set of allowed 32-bit values. The masking key needs to
> be unpredictable; thus, the masking key MUST be derived from a strong
> source of entropy, and the masking key for a given frame MUST NOT
> make it simple for a server/proxy to predict the masking key for a
> subsequent frame. The unpredictability of the masking key is
> essential to prevent authors of malicious applications from selecting
> the bytes that appear on the wire.

But if I'm reading the code right, autobahn appears to [select masks using random.getrandbits](https://github.com/crossbario/autobahn-python/blob/57c2f87d7750fa76165a06f2140b7f33f2f093d0/autobahn/websocket/protocol.py#L1800), which uses Python's default Mersenne Twister-based RNG. The Mersenne Twister is inappropriate for security-sensitive applications because its future outputs can be predicted by observing previous outputs. (I.e., seeing the first N masks lets you predict the future masks.)
Also, go through `git grep 'allennlp/run'` and update any references to the deprecated command.

This will let us use variable type annotations, and remove all of the `unused-imports` in the code.

I don't _think_ there are any big issues with just changing the python version in our images and build settings, so I'm labeling this as easy, but it's possible there is some library we're using that's not compatible and it will end up being hard.

 def predict(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
    predictor = get_predictor(args)
    output_file = None

    if args.silent and not args.output_file:
        print("--silent specified without --output-file.")
        print("Exiting early because no output will be created.")
        sys.exit(0)

    # ExitStack allows us to conditionally context-manage `output_file`, which may or may not exist
    with ExitStack() as stack:
        input_file = stack.enter_context(args.input_file)  # type: ignore
        if args.output_file:
            output_file = stack.enter_context(args.output_file)  # type: ignore

        run(predictor, input_file, output_file, not args.silent)
```

In my wrapper I am setting default values to **input_file, output_file and model_file**  as:

```
 parser.add_argument("archive_file", type=str, help="Model for SRL", default=srl_model_file)
 parser.add_argument("input_file", metavar="input-file", type=argparse.FileType('r'),
                        help="Path to input file.", default=input_file)
 parser.add_argument("--output-file", type=argparse.FileType('w'), help="Path to output file.", default=output_file)
 parser.set_defaults(func=predict)
```

and calling **func** as:

`args.func(args)`

But this code is throwing TypeError:

**TypeError: predict() takes 0 positional arguments but 1 was given**

Can someone help me to guide why I am getting TypeError, according to definition of **predict** it accepts one argument.

issue_title: How to duplicate a versionable object?
issue_body:
I have an object X, which is a Versionable model. I want to make a new object Y that has all the same field values as X, but has an unrelated history (i.e. no history). 

In projects without versioning, I can do this:

```python
o = MyModel.objects.get(name='x')
o.id = None
o.pk = None
o.save()
```

But a similar approach gets me an integrity error with CleanerVersion:

`IntegrityError: NOT NULL constraint failed: mymodel.id`

Any advice? I'll post back here when/if I find a solution. Thanks!

We are heading for a big release, containing:

- Refactor of locate to make it more modular. Some API breaks. (PR 400, 406
- Better performance of gray dilation (PR 361)
- Fix compute drift if dataframes are not sorted (PR 409)
- New refinement using least squares optimization (PR 407)
- New linker that combines gray dilation and linking `FindLinker` (PR 407, 410, 411, 416, 428)
- Refactor of the linking code to give the `Linker` and `FindLinker` a common code base and better API (PR 416)
- Fix double counting of non-linking penalty in non-numba linkers (PR 430)
- Fix the `N` column for `emsd` (PR 434)

On the milestone list are the following:

- [ ] Make `locate` even more modular #257
- [ ] Adjust default value of `smoothing_size` to the Crocker Grier value #401
- [ ] Finish the refactor of the linking code, breaking a part of the linking API (building on #416)
- [ ] Supply a parallelized `batch` function #304 
- [ ] Investigate skimage's moment computations #265 
- [ ] Investigate normalization questions raised in #239
- [ ] Read/write OpenPTV files #243 

I think we should finish the refactor of the linking code, better to change the API in one release. But for the rest, we could postpone until a later release. Thoughts? @danielballan @nkeim @tacaswell

issue_title: memory usage in locate and linking
issue_body:
Hello,
We've been trying to "process an unlimited number of frames" but hitting a hiccup with memory usage. Running in a jupyter notebook, the following eventually gobbles up all of the 128GB of our workstation while processing 200k frames with ~14k features per frame. Unfortunately, the scale needed to reproduce this also makes it difficult to provide a MWE. Any hints on what might be up or where to start with debugging?

```python
frames = pims.ImageSequence('/path/to/my/data/*.tif', as_grey=True)
start = time.time()
with tp.PandasHDFStore('/path/to/my/store.h5') as s:
   tp.batch(frames, 5, minmass=2, percentile=10, invert=False, characterize=False, output=s)
end = time.time()
print (end-start)
start = time.time()
with tp.PandasHDFStore('/path/to/my/store.h5') as s:
   for linked in tp.link_df_iter(s, 0.4, memory=5):
       s.put(linked)
end = time.time()
print (end-start)
```

The code made it through the feature finding, ok and then memory usage built during linking.
Using anaconda (py2.7) we're updated to the latest pandas, numba, trackpy, etc...

issue_title: Headers stream parameter should support multi line header?
issue_body:
Example file:
https://data.humdata.org/dataset/985b35e6-4bab-4332-af04-6b146be55eb5/resource/062b661b-77a2-4ce6-9676-6f0732c5714b/download/WFPVAM_MM35_Big-Table.xlsx

In the above file the headers are between rows 8 and 12 eg. for column F, the header consists of text in F9, F10 and F12. 

It would be good to be able to pass in a tuple or something representing the start and end row containing the header eg.
`stream = Stream('../WFPVAM_MM35_Big-Table.xlsx', headers=(8,12), fill_merged_cells=True)`

Thus with `headers=(8,12)`, column F's header should be read as "Seasonally adjusted quarterly change (% change)"

More complicated are the headers in columns L and M where L8 to M10 are merged and consist of one header, but there are subheaders underneath in L11-12 and M11-12.

With `headers=(8,12)`, column L's header should be "Cumulative impact of changes on cost of food basket from previous quarter"
With `headers=(8,12)`, column M's header should be "Cumulative impact of changes on cost of food basket from baseline (%)"

# Overview

Readme is expected to be in RST. See solution - https://github.com/frictionlessdata/jsontableschema-pandas-py

issue_title: Possible bug: permission issue preventing database restart
issue_body:
Testing #4414 prompted me to stop the database-- something I rarely do.  I noticed it sometimes won't restart after having been stopped.  I've verified that the same behavior is in master.

issue_body:
Memory mapped file will not gracefully provide an error if the user's machine runs out of memory. It will cause a BUS error.

See https://github.com/ethereum/cpp-ethereum/issues/806#issuecomment-101988058

issue_title: Add tests to prove we aren't leaving cruft in the db when things are reverted
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

While working on #569 I noticed it is far to easy to accidentally bypass the `JournalDB` altogether and still get the entire test suite to pass. I don't have a definite prove for that yet but it seems we don't have a test that actually tests that we aren't leaving cruft in the database when things are reverted.

I'm adding this issue as a reminder for me to follow up on that.

### How can it be fixed

Add missing tests

issue_title: Do not ignore data requests while performing DAO fork check
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Currently, while we're waiting for a remote to send us the DAO fork block headers in order to determine if we're on the same side of the fork, we ignore any data requests they make. That seems to cause them treat us as a useless peer and disconnect.

It'd explain why when running on ropsten trinity can keep a high peer count but on mainnet most nodes disconnect shortly after the initial handshake.

### How can it be fixed

Need to handle data requests while performing the DAO fork check. Or maybe keep ignoring them during the DAO fork check, but re-queue them once the check is completed

issue_title: Frequent peer disconnections during fast sync
issue_body:
Some geth peers disconnect from us on a frequent basis when we're doing a fast-sync, and they seem to do so because they're trying to fetch chain data from us and when we fail to respond too many times, they disconnect. See https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/blob/7cf83cee5240f76bdf8ff4f1024643fd126e1018/eth/downloader/downloader.go#L1083-L1090 and https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/blob/7cf83cee5240f76bdf8ff4f1024643fd126e1018/eth/downloader/downloader.go#L866-L869

I don't think there's much we can do about it as during a fast-sync we most likely won't have the data the peer is asking for, but thought I'd create this issue because others are likely to notice this as well.

### What is wrong?

Trying to sync the Ropsten chain (`python -m p2p.chain -db ~/path/to/db`) I noticed this exception coming up.

```
19:50:49 ERROR: Unexpected error during auth/p2p handshake with <Node(0x6332@52.232.243.152)>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/cburgdorf/Documents/hacking/ef/py-evm/p2p/peer.py", line 712, in connect
    timeout=HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT)
  File "/home/cburgdorf/Documents/hacking/ef/py-evm/p2p/cancel_token.py", line 99, in wait_with_token
    return done.pop().result()
  File "/home/cburgdorf/Documents/hacking/ef/py-evm/p2p/peer.py", line 110, in handshake
    ) = await auth.handshake(remote, privkey)
  File "/home/cburgdorf/Documents/hacking/ef/py-evm/p2p/auth.py", line 52, in handshake
    initiator, reader, writer)
  File "/home/cburgdorf/Documents/hacking/ef/py-evm/p2p/auth.py", line 71, in _handshake
    ephemeral_pubkey, responder_nonce = initiator.decode_auth_ack_message(auth_ack)
  File "/home/cburgdorf/Documents/hacking/ef/py-evm/p2p/auth.py", line 169, in decode_auth_ack_message
    raise ValueError("Auth ack msg too short: {}".format(len(ciphertext)))
ValueError: Auth ack msg too short: 0

issue_title: eth-utils dependency conflicts with other eth-* packages
issue_body:
* Version: 0.5.0
* Python: 3.5/3.6
* OS: osx/linux


### What was wrong?

Currently, the https://github.com/gitcoinco/web repository Travis builds are failing due to a dependency conflict with other `eth-` packages. 
Explanation: https://github.com/gitcoinco/web/issues/367

Validation is failing due to a conflict in dependencies:

- eth-keys and eth-keyfile pins eth-utils<2.0.0,>=1.0.0-beta.1
- eth-abi pins `eth-utils==0.7.*`

This seems to cause the build to fail dependency validation.

### How can it be fixed?

- Update `eth-abi` to use` >=1.0.0b1` of the `eth-utils` package?

#### Note

It appears `eth-tester` is resulting in the same error due to pinning `<1.0.0`

issue_title: Don't accept `str` as a primitive in `to_hex()`
issue_body:
* Version: 1.0.2
* Python: 3.5
* OS: osx/linux/win


### What was wrong?

`to_hex()` shouldn't presume the kind of `str` value in primitive. It's the kind of ambiguity we aim to avoid, using `hexstr` and `text` keyword args:
https://github.com/ethereum/eth-utils/blob/master/eth_utils/conversions.py#L41-L42

### How can it be fixed?

`to_hex()` should reject `str` values, by raising a TypeError and listing the accepted types: `(bytes, bytearray, int, bool)`

Double-check if this breaks anything in py-evm or web3.py

issue_title: Convenient access to (pre)terminals on Derivation tres
issue_body: If one wants to get the lexical entry or type of the leaves of a derivation tree, they must traverse the tree until a daughters list with a single UdfTerminal is found (since the preterminal is where lexical type and entry info is stored). Since this might be a likely usage of derivation trees, a more convenient function could be created to do this. E.g. `d.leaf_info()` or something, which returns a list of (lexical_entry, lexical_type, orth). Alternatively, `d.terminals()` and `d.preterminals()`, which could be more generic.

issue_title: allow automatic download of packages in non-default channels
issue_body:
Feature request

In my `conda` usage, I'm usually interested in downloading a package but I don't care what channel it's from. 

In the current state of `conda`, my workflow usually looks something like:

```bash
root@f6300fad4623: conda install tensorflow=1.0.0
Fetching package metadata .........

PackageNotFoundError: Package missing in current linux-64 channels: 
  - tensorflow 1.0.0*

```

I then have to go [anaconda cloud](https://anaconda.org/), search for the package, note the correct channel, then 

```bash
root@f6300fad4623: conda install -c conda-forge tensorflow=1.0.0
Fetching package metadata ...........
Solving package specifications: .

Package plan for installation in environment /opt/conda:

The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    funcsigs:   1.0.2-py27_0  conda-forge
    mkl:        2017.0.1-0               
    mock:       2.0.0-py27_0  conda-forge
    numpy:      1.12.1-py27_0            
    pbr:        3.0.0-py27_0  conda-forge
    protobuf:   3.3.0-py27_0  conda-forge
    tensorflow: 1.0.0-py27_0  conda-forge

The following packages will be SUPERSEDED by a higher-priority channel:

    conda:      4.3.17-py27_0             --> 4.2.13-py27_0 conda-forge
    conda-env:  2.6.0-0                   --> 2.6.0-0       conda-forge

Proceed ([y]/n)? 
```

I often have to repeat this several times when installing packages for a given project.

It would be ideal if there was an option that would allow 'automatic download of packages outside of default channels'. I am aware that I can [edit my `.condarc` config file](https://conda.io/docs/config.html#channel-locations-channels) to allow `conda` to search for packages in other channels, but given the number of channels it is difficult to anticipate in advance that I will need a package in a given channel. Is it possible for `conda` to automatically find the channels with the package I'm looking for?

The code above was run using [this](https://hub.docker.com/r/continuumio/miniconda/) docker image.

I don't think it should be possible to get a conda environment with Python and without pip. This is a source of frequent confusion, where people set up an env, pip install a few things, and realize (or don't) that pip is still associated with the system Python.

It is increasingly rare these days to have any Python installation without pip. It should be a safe assumption that if you have Python in an environment, pip is there, too.
Various lists of packages (e.g. download list when installing, cleanup list in `conda clean --all`, etc.) could be sorted alphabetically for a minor usability improvement.

issue_title: conda build also define C_INCLUDE_PATH and CPLUS_INCLUDE_PATH
issue_body:
"conda build" currently defines the environment variable INCLUDE_PATH=$PREFIX/include.  However, gcc doesn't use this, but instead uses C_INCLUDE_PATH and CPLUS_INCLUDE_PATH.

Because of this, all of my build.sh files look like this

C_INCLUDE_PATH=$PREFIX/include make install

Instead of the simpler

make install

Should these env vars also be defined?  Thanks

<!--
Hi!  Read this; it's important.

If your issue is a feature request for:

      ==> please file it here!
  * all other conda commands that start with 'conda':
      ==> please file it here!

-->

When in the course of conda events you sometimes run into

>UnsatisfiableError: The following specifications were found to be in conflict

Apt, on the other hand, says something like "well I could do it, but I'd have to make some other changes you may not like, but would allow me to proceed. (y/n/q)." I don't like that conda completely balks. 

What about adding a switch:

    balter$ conda install XXXX --please-dont-cop-out-and-give-up-please-suggest-a-solution
    OK balter, this is what I came up with. I can install XXXX, but I'll have to make the following changes:
    .
    .
    .

[mcgibbon@***.edu workspace]$ conda remove debug
[204525 refs]
[mcgibbon@***.edu workspace]$ conda list
[204525 refs]
[mcgibbon@***.edu workspace]$ conda
[204525 refs]
```

As a temporary work-around, how can I manually remove debug and go back to my previous Python installation? I am using Linux with a home directory install.

In the longer term, conda probably shouldn't break when installing packages in the anaconda channel.

issue_title: valid YAML recipe fails on conda build due to 'invalid YAML'
issue_body:
`conda 3.11.0`, using 

```
$ conda list
# packages in environment at /home/naught101/miniconda3/envs/ggplotdev:
#
anaconda-build            0.10.7                   py34_0  
anaconda-client           1.0.1                    py34_0

brewer2mpl                1.4                      py34_0  
...
python                    3.4.3                         1  
...
yaml                      0.1.6                         0  
zlib                      1.2.8                         0  
```

This recipe fails:
```{yaml}
$ cat recipe/meta.yaml
about: {home: 'https://github.com/jiffyclub/brewer2mpl/wiki', license: MIT License,
  summary: Connect colorbrewer2.org color maps to Python and matplotlib}
build:
  entry_points: []
  features: []
  script_env: []
  string: ''
  track_features: []
package: {name: brewer2mpl, version: '1.4'}
requirements:
  build: [python]
  conflicts: []
  run: [python]
source:
  fn: brewer2mpl-1.4.tar.gz
  git_rev: ''
  md5: 49b7f7de78a5a47021aee91bee543026
  patches: []
  path: ''
  svn_rev: ''
  url: https://pypi.python.org/packages/source/b/brewer2mpl/brewer2mpl-1.4.tar.gz
test:
  commands: []
  files: []
  imports: [brewer2mpl, brewer2mpl.wesanderson]
  requires: []
```

This is a meta.yaml *produced by `conda build`*. If I want to re-use it to build a package elsewhere, I can try, but it will fail:

```{bash}
$ conda build recipe
Error: Invalid selector in meta.yaml line 11:
  build: [python]
```

The YAML is perfectly valid, as far as I can see (it passes on http://www.yamllint.com/). `conda buil` fails on all such lines. I can only make it work by changing such lines to, e.g.:

```{yaml}
  imports: 
    - brewer2mpl
    - brewer2mpl.wesanderson
```

if I change the python line to:
```{yaml}
  build: python
```

it complains about there being no package 'p' available (presumably it's treating the string as a list..)

After some "digging", I found that the shebang generated by `pip` from outside the environment does not take the prefix argument into account : ``` $ head -1 /usr/local/miniconda3/envs/tensorFlow/bin/gpustat #!/usr/local/miniconda3/bin/python ``` instead of : `#!/usr/local/miniconda3/envs/tensorFlow/bin/python` What is my `pip` command missing ?

bgzf_getline strips line delimiters.

Here is test to verify this:

```
import pytest
import pysam
import tempfile


@pytest.fixture(params=[
    "A\nB\nC\n",
    "\nAAA\t",
    "AAA",
    ""])
def input_data(request):
    return request.param


def test_file(input_data):
    output_data = ""

    with pysam.BGZFile("test.txt", "wb") as file_descriptor:
        print("input:")
        for row in input_data.splitlines(True):
            print(repr(row))
            file_descriptor.write(row)
    with pysam.BGZFile("test.txt", "rb") as file_descriptor:
        print("output:")
        for row in file_descriptor:
            print(repr(row))
            output_data += row  # row should contain new line but it does not
    assert input_data == output_data, "input {} output {}".format(
        repr(input_data), repr(output_data))

```




First and second test fail

First

```
input:
'A\n'
'B\n'
'C\n'
output:
'A'
'B'
'C'
```

Second

input:

```
'\n'
'AAA\t'
output:
```

I also checked the temporary file look correct and it does. So the problem is in the for loop:

```
for row in file_descriptor:
    output_data += row
```

I believe the iterated row should contain the new row '\n'.

Hi, I am trying to connect to Elasticsearch. My problem is that I don't have a single CA Cert file (I have 3 files) and, because of that, I can't use `ca_certs` option in Elastic. I've decided to use `verity_certs=False`. This is my script:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python

from elasticsearch import Elasticsearch

es = Elasticsearch(
    ["https://my_host_url:9200"],
    http_auth=("elastic", "my_passwd"),
    use_ssl=True,
    verify_certs=False,
)

print es.info()
```

I don't know why it shows me the following error in line `print es.info()`:

```
elasticsearch.exceptions.SSLError: ConnectionError([Errno 2] No such file or directory) caused by: SSLError([Errno 2] No such file or directory)
```

Another solution I'm trying is using `connection_class` parameter to call [`HTTPSConnection`](http://urllib3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/index.html#urllib3.connection.VerifiedHTTPSConnection) class from `urllib3` and use `ca_cert_dir` parameter. This solution isn't working because `HTTPSConnection` class doesn't have the `perform_request` method defined.

issue_title: Support for Amazon AWS Hosted Elastic
issue_body:
Amazon's hosted elastic deployment requires that all requests to the service be signed using their protocol - provided we don't want to specify specific IPs in their access policy. In most production deployments, this would be the case. However, elasticsearch-py does not have the capability (or even the instrumentation) to allow the user to plugin a "Request Signer". A request signer takes all the parameters of the request and generates the additional headers that need to be added to the request.

We have locally written extensions to cope with this, alongwith a RequestSigner class that can sign elasticsearch requests appropriately to work with AWS. Given AWS' popularity and the structure of their hosted solution, it would be great if this can be supported natively in elasticsearch-py. Other hosted services might also require request signing in the future and hence generic pluggable Request Signing would be a useful addition.

issue_title: Allow user to specify RBAC on or off when installing aci connector
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The `az aks install-connector` command doesn't allow the user to toggle if the cluster has RBAC or not. This means it uses the default value of the virtual-kubelet helm chart: `rbac is disabled`.

This poses a problem for new AKS clusters that have RBAC. When the `az aks install-connector` command is used, they end up with virtual-kubelet in a broken state because the needed RBAC related resources are not created. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**

The `install-connector` command should have an additional flag to allow the user to enable or disable rbac for the vk install. 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

If we could determine if the cluster had RBAC, we could default this selection. 

**Additional context**

issue_title: TXT records set through SDK calls results in improper string value
issue_body:
I am using the DNSManagementClient to make record changes to zones in Azure. It seems there is a bug when creating TXT records. When making a TXT record with this SDK, the value of a TXT record is not stored as a string, but as a list of characters. This behavior shows up elsewhere and is almost definitely unintended.

Example SDK construction call in python function in a placeholder domain:
```
dns_client = DnsManagementClient(…)
dns_client.record_sets.create_or_update('TestResource1', 'banana.com', 'test_txt_SDK', 'TXT', {'ttl': 100, 'txt_records':[TxtRecord('sample string')]})
```
I also made this same record in the Azure online portal. Here are the results:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/29443197/29436378-0675da1e-8360-11e7-85e5-f7e764423cd6.png).


This is not just a cosmetic bug. We can retrieve this inconsistency when making calls to the zone. For example, here is what happens when we call the record using the SDK, as well as using dig:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/29443197/29436417-387b6718-8360-11e7-924c-768ae1425202.png)
(this is to a different record)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/29443197/29436542-dbe62cf8-8360-11e7-944f-6248d04d252e.png)


Furthermore, this bug also occurs when using the Azure CLI 2.0 to create the record. Making a TXT record through the Azure portal is the only method that has not returned this error.
In SDK for .NET, there is a mehod "FormatDeadLetterPath" to get dead-letter queue path, but seems missing here.

issue_title: Can't work with China cloud when using connection string
issue_body:
REPRO WITH:

```
from azure.storage.blob import BlockBlobService
connection_string = 'A_CONNECTION_STRING_FOR_CHINA_STORAGE_ACCOUNT'
endpoint_suffix = '.core.chinacloudapi.cn'
client = BlockBlobService(connection_string=connection_string, endpoint_suffix=endpoint_suffix)
containers = list(client.list_containers())
print(containers)
```

The script will hang has it attempts to use the endpoint `.blob.core.windows.net` (even though endpoint_suffix is for China cloud) and the storage account at that endpoint does not exist.

issue_title: Requests on dropped connections are always fulfilled
issue_body:
## Long story short

Requests to the `web.Application` are answered even if the clients already dropped their connections before the web handlers are started.

It is important to fix this because under load the resources are being used for closed connections instead of serving the ones that are currently open.

## Expected behaviour

I would expect web handlers to not be executed if their connections are already dropped.

Notice, that I am not talking about connections that are dropped while processing web handlers as discussed in https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/issues/2098.

## Actual behaviour

Web handlers are started for already dropped connections.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Run a load test with a simple server that simulates some synchronous work like

```bash
wrk -d1m -c100 -t10 http://127.0.0.1:3001
Running 1m test @ http://127.0.0.1:3001
  10 threads and 100 connections
  Thread Stats   Avg      Stdev     Max   +/- Stdev
    Latency     1.08s   573.01ms   1.84s    66.67%
    Req/Sec     0.82      0.77     3.00     59.39%
  197 requests in 1.00m, 33.86KB read
  Socket errors: connect 0, read 0, write 0, timeout 191
Requests/sec:      3.28
Transfer/sec:     577.13B
```

 on

```python
import logging
import time
from aiohttp import web
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG,
                    format='%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(message)s')
i = 0
async def index(_):
    global i; i += 1; print(i)
    time.sleep(0.3)
    return web.json_response({'success': True})

app = web.Application()
app.router.add_get('/', index)
web.run_app(app, host='0.0.0.0', port=3001)
```

and observe that most of the requests have sockets that are timing out (191/197) and that the server continues to work past the load test. Fulfilling a total of about 400 requests.

2. Alternatively, you can increase the sleep time (say 30s) and create a telnet session with `GET / HTTP/1.1` and while this request is waiting, just open another telnet session with a similar request and close it before the first request is completed. You will see that the second request will always be fulfilled, and the web handler will be executed twice.

## Your environment

aiohttp version: 1fd798b69c0d04dcd913018002ca3cf729b089b4 (HEAD -> master)
on macOS 10.13.3 with Python 3.6.4 from Homebrew

issue_title: Deprecate low-level api from aiohttp namespace
issue_body:
Deprecate 

`aiohttp.HttpMessage`
`aiohttp.Response`
`aiohttp.Request`
 `aiohttp.RawRequestMessage`
`aiohttp.RawResponseMessage`
 `aiohttp.HttpRequestParser`
`aiohttp.HttpResponseParser`
`aiohttp.HttpPayloadParser`

`aiohttp.HttpProcessingError`
`aiohttp.BadHttpMessage`
`aiohttp.HttpBadRequest`
`aiohttp.BadStatusLine`
`aiohttp.LineTooLong`
`aiohttp.InvalidHeader`

`aiohttp.ClientDisconnectedError`

## Long story short

Is there any pretty way to handle async function after websocket handler cancellation? I've created ugly solutions like:
```
async def wrapper():
    async with request.db_conn.cursor() as cursor:
        await cursor.execute('UPDATE `nodes` SET `status`=%s WHERE `id`=%s', ('DISCONNECTED', node_id))

try:
    await asyncio.shield(wrapper())
except CancelledError:
    pass
```
or putting update action into the event queue handled in different task. 


## Expected behaviour

```
@routes.get('/ws')
async def handler(request):
    logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

    response = web.WebSocketResponse()
    await response.prepare(request)

    async with request.db_conn.cursor() as cursor:
        await cursor.execute('INSERT INTO `clients` (`status`) VALUES (%s);', ('CONNECTED',))
        node_id = cursor.lastrowid

    request.app.websockets[response] = node_id
    logger.debug('Client connected')

    async for msg in response:
        logger.debug(f'Message: {msg}')

        if msg.type == WSMsgType.TEXT:
            data = json.loads(msg.data)
            
        elif msg.type == WSMsgType.ERROR:
            logger.error(f'Connection closed with exception {response.exception()}')

    # CancelledError
    async with request.db_conn.cursor() as cursor:
        await cursor.execute('UPDATE `clients` SET `status`=%s WHERE `id`=%s', ('DISCONNECTED', node_id)) 

    del request.app.websockets[response]
    logger.debug('Client disconnected')

    return response
```

## Actual behaviour

await cursor.execute raises CancelledError

From my understanding on `app.on_startup` the loop is already present.
Why do we need additional signal?
`app.on_loop_available` is not documented BTW
@fafhrd91 ?

issue_title: Move ssl-related params from TCPConnector to request args
issue_body:
## Long story short

Now ssl configuration is tightly coupled with `TCPConnector`.

It is not convenient (user should create a connector and pass it into session) and disallows to use the same `ClientSession` for working with different ssl contexts etc.

I think we should move `verify_ssl`, `fingerprint` and `ssl_context` into `ClientSession.request` and `ClientSession.ws_connect` like we have done for `proxy` and `proxy_auth` (see #998).
Current TCPConnector's parameters should be kept as default values which may be overridden by request without any warning/error.

issue_title: Unable to establish websocket connection behind nginx reverse proxy
issue_body:
I have a Digital Ocean server running Ubuntu 14.04, with Jupyter Notebook (https://hub.docker.com/r/jupyter/notebook/) running inside a Docker container. I also have the nginx-proxy (https://github.com/jwilder/nginx-proxy) Docker container to serve as my reverse proxy.

I have it configured such that notes.rooday.com would be pointed to the Jupyter container, and that works as intended. I'm able to view the application, authenticate, make and edit files, etc. However, attempting to run an ipython notebook file gives a Connection Failed error:

```
A connection to the notebook server could not be established. The notebook will continue trying to reconnect, but until it does, you will NOT be able to run code. Check your network connection or notebook server configuration.
```

Looking inside the Chrome console, I see this error:

```
WebSocket connection to 'ws://notes.rooday.com/api/kernels/251adc99-3c1b-4d97-a9ae-9ecbfba3bc70/channels?session_id=28DC42BB11FD413A86D75BAE570CA379' failed: Error during WebSocket handshake: Unexpected response code: 400
```

The kernel attempts to reconnect, but to no avail. Checking the docker logs for the nginx proxy yields the following relevant lines:

```
nginx.1    | notes.rooday.com 172.17.0.1 - - [06/Apr/2016:21:42:16 +0000] "GET /api/kernels/007c6099-febe-40b3-847c-5d90bcc4cf4b/channels?session_id=22F4BC7FDFCD49A28948903EA2B6ACED HTTP/1.1" 400 34 "-" "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/49.0.2623.110 Safari/537.36"
nginx.1    | notes.rooday.com 172.17.0.1 - - [06/Apr/2016:21:42:17 +0000] "GET /api/kernels/007c6099-febe-40b3-847c-5d90bcc4cf4b/channels?session_id=22F4BC7FDFCD49A28948903EA2B6ACED HTTP/1.1" 400 34 "-" "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/49.0.2623.110 Safari/537.36"
nginx.1    | notes.rooday.com 172.17.0.1 - - [06/Apr/2016:21:42:19 +0000] "GET /api/kernels/007c6099-febe-40b3-847c-5d90bcc4cf4b/channels?session_id=22F4BC7FDFCD49A28948903EA2B6ACED HTTP/1.1" 400 34 "-" "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/49.0.2623.110 Safari/537.36"
```

and the logs for the Jupyter container show

```
[I 21:20:08.864 NotebookApp] 302 GET / (172.17.0.2) 3.53ms
[I 21:20:08.893 NotebookApp] 302 GET /tree (172.17.0.2) 4.38ms

```

Based on this, and asking in the nginx-proxy issues page, it would seem that Jupyter is the cause of the problem. The nginx proxy is configured to allow websocket connections, but they're still not going through. Has anyone else attempted to run Jupyter behind a reverse proxy, or know enough to help me find a workaround?

issue_title: built-in words are used as variable and argument names
issue_body:
For example, in https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/master/notebook/services/contents/filemanager.py#L320

both `type` and `format` are remapped as argument names

builtins:
```
In [1]: format(5) # formats to str
Out[1]: '5'

In [1]: type(5) # returns type of the object
Out[1]: int
```

issue_title: My os.environ don't see my actual variables from Jupyter notebook

issue_title: Should symlink option in nbextensions API fall back to copy?
issue_body:
`install_nbextension()` takes a `symlink=` boolean parameter, which instructs it to symlink the extension rather than copying it. In most cases this will fail on Windows, and the docstring says as much.

If symlink fails, should it automatically fall back on copying (the default without `symlink=True`)?

In favour: this is what most extensions probably want, and if we don't have a fallback, they have to have that fallback themselves.

Against: some extensions may rely on it actually making a symlink (e.g. symlink a directory into nbextensions, then change the contents of that directory). Automatic fallbacks make it harder to know what's really happening.

From ipython/ipython#8440

issue_body: Diploid inheritence, mendel errors, etc.

See the following test case:

```
import autograd.numpy as np

f = autograd.value_and_grad(fn1)

Ndarrays containing 32 bit floats have half the memory footprint of 64 bit, and operations on them are also significantly faster (I expect this is different on different systems but the speedup seems to be around 8x for basic operations on my computer, using numpy with OpenBLAS). At the moment we have
```python
>>> import autograd
>>> import autograd.numpy as np

```

I'm pretty sure this is because the `1.0` value on [this line]( https://github.com/HIPS/autograd/blob/master/autograd/core.py#L49) defaults to a float64, so it should be pretty easy to properly support `float32` by testing the type of the value of the endnode and copying that type.

issue_title: planemo travis_init should use planemo lint
issue_body:
The ``planemo travis_init`` command at its core will setup a TravisCI configuration which via ``.travis.yml`` will download and run https://github.com/galaxyproject/planemo/blob/master/scripts/travis_test.sh meaning any changes to the master script will immediately take effect on third party Galaxy tool testing.

Right now, apart from the Galaxy and Tool installation magic, the key command is ``planemo test``.

I am proposing that at the start of ``travis_test.sh`` we invoke ``planemo shed_lint`` with ``--fail_level error`` (rather than the more stringent default ``--fail_level warn`` which would likely cause many false positives) as already used in https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blob/master/.travis.yml and https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-devteam/blob/master/.travis.yml

The lint command is quick, and will flush out problems which can be caught without wasting donated VM run-time on running the test suite.

issue_title: Enable parallel build if support by underlying build tool
issue_body:
* If Makefile Unix, Ninja or Jom based generated are used:  `-j<number_of_core>`
* If Visual Studio:
 * `/MP<number_of_core>` 
 * `/maxcpucount:<number_of_core>` 

Note that of complex project, it could be hard to find the right setting for `/MP` and `/maxcpucount`. For example, you want to avoid too many link steps requiring a lot of memory to happen at the same time ....  Having a way to disable parallel build may be a sensible option.

More details:
* With VS >= 2010, target level parallelism can be set from command line using  `/maxcpucount`. (Number of simultaneous MSBuild.exe processes). See [1]
* object level parallelism set using `/MP`   (Number of simultaneous cl.exe processes). See [2]

[1] https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/bb651793%28v=vs.120%29.aspx
[2] https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/bb385193.aspx

Here is some mode details of what we did for Slicer: http://massmail.spl.harvard.edu/public-archives/slicer-devel/2016/019656.html

issue_title: When parsing multiple sources with same parser, database object is shared
issue_body:
When the same parser is used to parse multiple source files, the database object is shared and only a reference is returned. This has some (at least to me) unexpected consequences. So far I have run into:

1. If `entries_dict` is used, `_entries_dict` already exists and is not overwritten even though `entries` have changed. In such a case, `entries` and `entries_dict` do not match.
2. A parsed database is overwritten if the object is not explicitly copied

Is this intended behavior? To me it feels more natural if the parser creates a new database object every time a new source file is parsed. 

I have forked and created a hacky solution by moving database initialization to the parse function. It works for my purposes, but I don't fully understand how and why the database object is referenced internally in the parser, so I'm not sure this is a good idea.

Thanks for a great package, by the way!

### Example of 1

```python
from bibtexparser.bparser import BibTexParser

parser = BibTexParser()

db1 = parser.parse("""
@article{article1,
    title = {Article 1}
}
""")

print("db1.entries contains: {}".format(', '.join(e["ID"] for e in db1.entries)))
print("db1.entries_dict contains: {}".format(', '.join(db1.entries_dict.keys())))

db2 = parser.parse("""
@article{article2,
    title = {Article 2}
}
""")

print("db2.entries contains: {}".format(', '.join(e["ID"] for e in db2.entries)))
print("db2.entries_dict contains: {}".format(', '.join(db2.entries_dict.keys())))
```
Gives the output:

```
db1.entries contains: article1
db1.entries_dict contains: article1
db2.entries contains: article2
db2.entries_dict contains: article1
```

### Example of 2

```python
from bibtexparser.bparser import BibTexParser

parser = BibTexParser()

db1 = parser.parse("""
@article{article1,
    title = {Article 1}
}
""")

db2 = parser.parse("""
@article{article2,
    title = {Article 2}
}
""")


print("db1.entries contains: {}".format(', '.join(e["ID"] for e in db1.entries)))
print("db1.entries_dict contains: {}".format(', '.join(db1.entries_dict.keys())))

print("db2.entries contains: {}".format(', '.join(e["ID"] for e in db2.entries)))
print("db2.entries_dict contains: {}".format(', '.join(db2.entries_dict.keys())))
```
Gives the output:

```
db1.entries contains: article2
db1.entries_dict contains: article2
db2.entries contains: article2
db2.entries_dict contains: article2
```

issue_title: `endpoint.{name}.joined` only removed in hook _after_ relation-broken hook
issue_body:
The docs say the following

> the `joined` flag will be automatically cleared when there are no remote units remaining on any relation, but neither of the other flags will be automatically cleared.

I read this as "the `joined` flag will be removed **when the last unit of the last relationship is `departed`**". So for an endpoint with one relation and one remote unit, the `joined` flag will be removed at the same time that the `departed` flag is set.

This is however not the case: the `joined` flag is only removed when during the hook _after_ the  `relation-broken` hook because that is the first time when Juju says that there are no relations/units connected anymore.

Look at this output. The desired behavior is that the `joined` flag is removed in the first `departed` hook, not in the `update-status` hook.

``` bash
# Departed hook
2018-01-18 13:16:30 INFO juju-log kubernetes-request:94: Reactive main running for hook kubernetes-request-relation-departed
2018-01-18 13:16:31 INFO juju-log kubernetes-request:94: Invoking reactive handler: reactive/kubernetes-resource.py:11:print_flags

issue_title: In Python 3.6 unused variables in f-strings show line number 1
issue_body:
I don't know if this is fixable or not, but in Python 3.6, f-strings with unused variables show the line number as 1. This is very annoying as I use an editor plugin that highlights lines that have errors. 

The issue seems to be fixed in Python 3.7. 

```
$cat test.py
def test():
    f"{b}"
```

**Python 3.6**

```
$PYTHONPATH=~/Documents/pyflakes python -m pyflakes test.py
test.py:1: undefined name 'b'
```

**Python 3.7**
```
$PYTHONPATH=~/Documents/pyflakes python -m pyflakes test.py
test.py:2: undefined name 'b'
```

issue_title: Subtle output flushing issue in Python 3.4
issue_body:
I just tracked down and annoying behavior change that we got in using Pyflakes when we upgraded from Python 2 to Python 3 in the Zulip project.  It's related to interleaving stdout and stderr from pyflakes.  

Basically we have a little pyflakes wrapper, `check_pyflakes`, that excludes certain warnings we're not interested in, by sending stdout and stderr to a pipe.  It suddenly stopped filtering lines correctly when we switched to Python 3, because we were interleaving the stdout/stderr output from `pyflakes`, and apparently in Python 3, the newline characters in the pyflakes output got interleaved wrong, and so the filtering filtered the wrong thing.  i.e. with code like `p = subprocess.Pipe(["pyflakes", ...], stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT); for ln in p.readlines(): print(p)`, you'll end up with lines of pyflakes output like this:

`b"zproject/settings.py:95: 'from .prod_settings import *' used; unable to detect undefined nameszerver/views/users.py:49:39: invalid syntax\n"`

(there should have been a `\n` in `nameszerver`).

Here's the commit I just merged into Zulip working around this issue, with details on the specifics:  https://github.com/zulip/zulip/commit/6de77db93fe20d747541309d952035f070a157f6

Since I have found that needing to add a `sys.stdout.flush()` is a common type of new bug when switching to Python 3, I figured I'd report this, since I think the reason this stopped working is that Pyflakes needs such a statement added somewhere, and fixing this might save other folks moving to Python 3 some pain.

I'm happy to provide more details on how to reproduce, but I think it should be easy to do with the `subprocess` invocation that we have in the pre-6de77db9 world.

Thanks for a great tool, and I hope this report helps!

issue_title: Browser dies when Tornado server is killed, causes system to register application 'crash'
issue_body:
Not sure if this is desired functionality, but when the server is killed or the connection is closed **and** the visualization is the only tab in the browser, the browser is closed. Ideally this would be fine, but Chrome throws an error when you launch it independent of the server/visualization, asking to Restore as if Chrome had crashed.

This is a relatively minor issue, but it could give the user the impression that not everything is okey-dokey with their system.

issue_title: Incorrect failure message attribute in junitxml file when combined with xdist
issue_body:
When combining xdist with pytest's junitxml flag there is an issue where the generated report will have the incorrect <failure message=> attribute value. 

test_case.py

```python
import pytest

class TestxDistXmlIssue:
    def test_one(self):
        # stack trace code
        # stack trace code
        # stack trace code
        # stack trace code
        # stack trace code

        # stack trace code
        # stack trace code
        # stack trace code
        # stack trace code
        assert False, "Only print this message"
```

```
pytest -n2 --junitxml="test_xdist.xml" test_case.py
```

```xml

    </testcase>
    <testcase classname="pytesting.test_case.TestxDistXmlIssue" file="pytesting/test_case.py" line="17" name="test_one" time="0.0014431476593017578">
        <failure message="self = <test_case.TestxDistXmlIssue object at 0x7fab8351fac8>      def test_one(self):         # stack trace code         # stack trace code         # stack trace code         # stack trace code         # stack trace code >       assert 1 == 2, "Only print this message" E       AssertionError: Only print this message E       assert 1 == 2  pytesting/test_case.py:24: AssertionError">self = <test_case.TestxDistXmlIssue object at 0x7fab8351fac8>

    def test_one(self):
        # stack trace code

pytesting/test_case.py:24: AssertionError</failure>

        <failure message="AssertionError: Only print this message assert 1 == 2">self = <test_case.TestxDistXmlIssue object at 0x7f63ae6656d8>

    def test_one(self):
        # stack trace code

>       assert 1 == 2, "Only print this message"
E       AssertionError: Only print this message
E       assert 1 == 2

The current backoff algorithm is quite effective, but I think it would benefit from supporting configuration of the 'cap' used for the time limit. issue_title: Ambiguous choice of directory name for SAN certs leads to wrong cert served
issue_body:
Steps to reproduce (from memory):

1. Use `letsencrypt --server https://acme-staging.api.letsencrypt.org/directory auth` to issue and install a cert for two names `jacob.hoffman-andrews.com hoffman-andrews.com`, using the staging instance
2. Use `letsencrypt --server <prod> auth` to issue and install from production for the two names `hoffman-andrews.com jacob.hoffman-andrews.com`. Note the different order!

Expected result:

Apache serves the prod cert.

Actual result:

Apache serves the staging cert.

Upon further investigation, I found that the staging cert existed in /etc/letsencrypt/live/jacob.hoffman-andrews.com/cert.pem and that prod cert existed in /etc/letsencrypt/live/hoffman-andrews.com/cert.pem.

My Apache config had:

```
SSLCertificateFile /etc/letsencrypt/live/jacob.hoffman-andrews.com/cert.pem
```

So it was still pointed at the first, staging cert, rather than being updated to point at the new prod cert.

issue_title: multiple -d options are ignored in letsencrypt-auto -- only the first one works
issue_body:
I tried to script some things and I have 5 subdomains .. when I found that when i do

``letsencrypt-auto certonly -d subd1 -d subd2 -d subd3 ..``

after the process completed, I only had a cert for subd1.

Unless I am missing something, that seems like a bug. I tried to find it here to see if it's open already, however, a quick search did not seem to indicate this was known already.

After issuing a new cert, the client will get a link to the issuer cert using a link with rel="up". We expect that there will be multiple "up" links for the multiple intermediate certs. One intermediate cert will be signed by the ISRG Root. The other intermediate cert will be signed by the IdenTrust Root. In production rollout we'll want the client to choose the IdenTrust-signed intermediate, which will be trusted by real clients.

The client should probably fetch each issuer cert listed in an "up" link, parse it, and prioritize based on that intermediate's Issuer field, comparing it against a baked-in list of roots that it knows to be widely trusted.

issue_body: If the user ran "letsencrypt certonly" and then runs "letsencrypt install", it's very counter-intuitive that they need to find their cert in `/etc/letsencrypt/live`, know that they should use `fullchain.pem` rather than `cert.pem`, and then find the matching key in `/etc/letsencrypt/keys`.  We use all of the storage.py mechanisms by default.

issue_title: Standalone authenticator doesn't properly shut down web server on error exit
issue_body:
Steps to reproduce:

Run

`letsencrypt --server SERVER_PATH --no-verify-ssl --authenticator standalone --text --agree-eula --agree-tos -d 1.le-test.hoffman-andrews.com auth  `

Ensure the authentication fails somehow. In my case it specifically failed with a 400 in POST /acme/challenge/..... One way to accomplish a similar thing would be to run a local Boulder, do a successful LE run (to set up the registration), then bring down Boulder and try another LE run.

Then run another authorization request.

Expected result:

Success, or another failure.

Actual result:

`The program letsencrypt (process ID 12717) is already listening on TCP port 443.
This will prevent us from binding to that port. Please stop the letsencrypt
program temporarily and then try again.`

issue_title: When connected to two down brokers, error_cb only trigger KafkaError._TRANSPORT
issue_body:
When there is only one broker, error_cb will trigger KafkaError._TRANSPORT and KafkaError._ALL_BROKERS_DOWN：

```
from confluent_kafka import Producer, KafkaError, KafkaException

def error_cb(err):
    print 'error_cb -------->', err
    if err.code() == KafkaError._ALL_BROKERS_DOWN:
        print 'all brokers down...'

KAFKA_BROKERS = '192.168.7.158:9092'
KAFKA_TOPIC = 'hermes-alarm-messages'
KAFKA_PRODUCER_CONF = {
    'api.version.request': False,  # default, not need to set (yet)

    'bootstrap.servers': KAFKA_BROKERS,
    'client.id': 'message_service',
    'default.topic.config': {'acks': 'all'},
    'error_cb': error_cb,

}
KAFKA_PRODUCER = Producer(**KAFKA_PRODUCER_CONF)
print '======>'
KAFKA_PRODUCER.poll(1)
```

issue_title: pack file almost seven times larger than the source code
issue_body: The .git/objects/pack/ directory is 740Mb, while the rest of the repository is 119 Mb.  Most likely this is due to historical records that may or may not be needed.  This repository took an inordinate amount of time to fork (or clone).  I would suggest that the maintainers at least look into the possibility of pruning the history to create a more reasonable sized repository.  If this is considered a reasonable request (there may be good reasons why it is not), a google search on 'git pack file large' should offer some avenues to explore.

issue_title: tz.gettz broken when time zone not found
issue_body:
When we switched over to using weak references for caching (see #635), we didn't take into account the fact that occasionally `tz.gettz` returns `None` (when no `tzinfo` object is found). Since you cannot have a weak reference to `None`, when we try to populate the weak cache, it raises an error.

This can be reproduced with:

```python
from dateutil import tz
tz.gettz('🐼')
```

The result:
> TypeError: cannot create weak reference to 'NoneType' object

This is causing failures in pandas' test suite: https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/issues/22337
