issue_body:
Hi guys,

I am currently struggling with [Lead-->Contact conversions](https://www.salesforce.com/developer/docs/api/Content/sforce_api_calls_convertlead.htm). I would like to do it from Django but it seems some fields related to this conversion are not writable. Namely `Lead.is_converted` and `Lead.converted_contact` have this `sf_read_only=models.NOT_UPDATEABLE` set.

Do you know how to deal with this Lead-->Contact conversion with `django-salesforce` properly?

Currently I have been trying to get it right with the following function

```python
def convert_lead(lead):
    """convert_lead
    Re-implement the `convertLead()` function using the Salesforce
    REST API. At the time this is written, `convertLead()` is only
    available through the SOAP (Apex) API.

    From the official documentation:

    > Convert a Lead into an Account and Contact, as well as (optionally)
    > an Opportunity.

    For the sake of this application, we only need to convert the Lead
    into a Contact.

    This function returns the newly created Contact.
    """
    if not lead.is_converted:
        # Convert the Lead into a Contact
        lead_serial = LeadSerializer(lead)
        contact_serial = ContactSerializer(data=lead_serial.data)

        if contact_serial.is_valid():
            c = contact_serial.object
            c.pk = None  # to avoid using the pk of the Lead instance
            c.save()
            lead.is_converted = True
            lead.status = 'Qualified - converted'
            lead.converted_contact = c
            lead.save()  # this fails (silently) to save the updated lead because of sf_read_only=models.NOT_UPDATEABLE on some fields
            return c
        else:
            # Should not happen
            raise RuntimeError(
                "The provided Lead could not be converted into a Contact.")
    else:
        # This Lead is already converted. Abort the mission.
        raise RuntimeWarning(
            "This Lead has already been converted.")
```

All the best.

issue_body:
I'm creating a map with layers. If I filter the dataset to be small I noticed that layers are displayed but if I don't filter it then the layers are not displayed. I there was a way around this limitation?

Here's how I'm creating the map (df is a dataframe from pandas). The layers are being created by analyzing the unique values in the "opp" column.

```python
ddfg = defaultdict()
for opp in df['opp'].unique():
    ddfg[opp] = folium.FeatureGroup(name=opp)

mymap = folium.Map(location=[lat_mean, lon_mean], zoom_start=10, tiles='Stamen Toner')

    color = color_status(status)

I strive to have powerline display the current date in my tmux status line. To do so, I included the following segment in my tmux theme configuration at `.config/powerline/themes/tmux/custom.json`

    { 
      "function": "powerline.segments.common.time.date", 
      "name": "date", 
      "args": { 
        "format": "%x" 
      } 
    }

The `%x` format specifier to python's `time.strftime` function should render the date according to the locale, which would today be `11.03.2018` in my locale `de_DE.UTF-8`. But instead, `03/11/18` appears, which looks like it was rendered in the generic `C` locale.

Testing the entry in the zsh theme yields the same result.

The installation is an Arch Linux booting systemd. Its `/etc/locale.conf` reads just

    LANG=de_DE.utf8

Running `locale` outputs

    LANG=de_DE.utf8
    LC_CTYPE="de_DE.utf8"

    LC_COLLATE="de_DE.utf8"

issue_title: Meta option `unknown` is no inherited on inherited Schema
New feature for iterating over fields_dict in marshalling/unmarshalling
issue_body:
jsonschema(and swagger) have specific options for unexpected additional fields.
- [additionalProperties](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#model-with-mapdictionary-properties)
- [patternProperties(maybe, jsonschema only)](http://json-schema.org/example2.html#base-schema)

And, I want to translate this feature to marshmallow schema.

## marshmallow's current implementation

The way of iteration in marshmallow's current implementation, is just calling `iteritems()` with fields_dict. So, it is difficult that define schema allowing unexpected field.

for examples, in swagger (openAPI2.0) the schema definition, like bellow.

```yaml
definitions:
  person:
    type: object
    properties:
      name:
        type: string
    additionalPropeties: integer
    required:
      - name
```

Then, about person schema, passing `{"name": "foo"}` is ok, and `{"name": "foo", "x": 20, "y": 10}` is also ok(unexpected field is treated as integer).

it is a bit difficult, making marshmallow's version of this.
(schema meta's additional is candidate of expected field names, ... and so on.)

## proposal

If marshmallow has new hook point that changing the way of iteration, it is enable to implement marshmallow's version, more easily.

For example, `get_marshalling_iterator()`(this is not good name), is existed in Schema class.

```python
# def default_iterator(data, fields_dict):
#     return iteritems(fields_dict)


class S(Schema):
    def get_marshalling_iterator(self, additional_field=fields.Int()):
        def iterate_with_additional_field(data, fields_dict):
            for k in data:
                field = fields_dict.get(k, additional_field)
                yield k, field

        return iterate_with_additional_field

    name = fields.String(required=True)
```

- 💭 get_marshalling_iterator is not good name, maybe
- 💭 the position of hook point(Schema class's instance method) is not good, maybe

Is it correct to say that marshmallow *marshals* objects into dicts which can then be *encoded* using msgpack, json, protobuf, etc?  Further, the marshalling and encoding are both part of the serialization process?

Is it possible to have a `Nested` field that does not allow creating new nested objects, but only allows lookups? Let me explain.

Consider the following schemas:

```python
class IndicatorSchema(ModelSchema):
    class Meta:
        model = Indicator
        sqla_session = db.session
        fields = ('id', 'code', 'name')

class RatingSchema(ModelSchema):
    indicator = Nested(IndicatorSchema)

    class Meta:
        model = Rating
        sqla_session = db.session
        fields = ('id', 'rating', 'indicator')
```

This would allow creating a rating AND indicator like so:
```python
schema = RatingSchema()

data = {'rating': 5, 'indicator': {'code': 4, 'name': 'Test Indicator'}}

rv = schema.load(data)
```

I want to prevent that and only allow indicators to be existing items:

```python

# indicator with ID 123 must exist, otherwise there will be an error
data = {'rating': 5, 'indicator': {'id': 123}}

rv = schema.load(data)
```

Is this possible, or am I going to need to write something custom to accomplish this?

Hello Django-tastypie security,

I sent an email a week ago to the security email address: tastypie-security@googlegroups.com but I have not heard a response, so I am posting my email here.

My name is Peter Robinson and I am a product security expert at Intel working in Longmont, Colorado.

I cloned your latest git repository at: https://github.com/django-tastypie/django-tastypie

I ran your latest version of Django-tastypie, version 0.13.3  through our Static Code Analyzer  (SCA) tool.

I wanted to report to you the findings of our SCA tool. The SCA tool that we are using is called Checkmarx

Please see my “Conclusions” remarks after each potential issue listed below.

I am interested in hearing your thoughts about each of the potential vulnerability issues.

Thanks for your time ,

Peter


Checkmarx found 2 High Vulnerabilities and 1 Low Vulnerability.

Because of the nature of a static code analysis tools in general, false positives may be found.


Scan Results Details

1 - Reflected XSS All Clients

 
![1](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20309295/23366555/5b8339de-fcc4-11e6-99be-e6f036ccf6e5.png)


Conclusion:  In the api function: top_level, lines 158 to 166 could potentially be used to have a defined javascript function run resulting in
cross site scripting (Reflected). This is highly unlikely.

From the https://www.quora.com/What-is-the-difference-between-request-get-and-request-GET-get-in-django:

request.GET is a dictionary-like object containing all GET request parameters. This is specific to Django.
The method get() returns a value for the given key if key is in the dictionary. If key is not available then returns default value None. Read more here.
request.GET.get() is combination of above two using which key/values of a GET request can be accessed. This would be mostly used in Django Views.



2 – Second order SQL Injection

 
![2](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20309295/23366559/5f1f8fc0-fcc4-11e6-87fe-17d68af71f01.png)


Conclusion:  This is most likely not a valid  security vulnerability because this is the prescribed method of writing custom Django admin commands from Django.
see: https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.10/howto/custom-management-commands/
In this case, the command would be run with: python manage.py backfill_api_keys
A user cannot “easily”  influence the result from the python list “User = get_user_model()”to cause SQL injection.
However, this would depend on how the ApiKey is used and whether or not a user can replace his own ApiKey with an arbitrary value.


3 – Information Exposure through an Error Message
![3](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/20309295/23366562/629b2132-fcc4-11e6-8080-8abc546209c3.png)

 
Conclusion:  This appears to be a valid error handling condition. Checkmarx is concerned that too much information is revealed to a user.
This is a Low vulnerability issue.

Right now `get_identifier` will return the ip:host of the current request, other times it will return the current username or 'nouser' if the request isn't authenticated.

This should be standardized to always be the username or the ip:host.

issue_title: 几个问题：1. 支持sparse-dnn训练吗，现在实测能够支持sparse多大维度，看到paper里好像都是图片的 2. allreduce怎么支持的，是数据分成N片后，然后发到对应的N台机器，再循环分发吗
different learning rate for weight and bias
issue_body: set different learning rate for weight and bias

Neural nets are not only used for dense inputs such as image and audio. Sparse inputs should also be supported. 

Now there's a trend to use NN for recommendation (e.g. wide&deep recommendation, ncf, rnn-based recommender). Many recommender works on dataframes (columns of features) and in such cases, sparse inputs are quite common because of heavily used one-hot encoding. 

We need to support Sparse Tensor as input for mostly used layers.  As for which layers we need to support for Sparse Input, I think for now we can just support the layers that are used in Wide&Deep recommender.  Then we can add support for new layers per request from customers.

issue_title: Segfault when string in algebraic datatype
issue_body:
```smt2
(declare-datatypes () ((Val (Str (str String)))))
```
causes a segmentation fault on current master.

In this example Z3 seems to get into an infinite loop in proto_model::eval, quickly exhausting memory. I would recommend running this in the debug version. Otherwise you have to kill it quickly to avoid thrashing your machine. It builds up a very long 'todo' list in proto_model::eval, which makes me think it might have built a cyclic expression node. If you remove the line '(push)' Z3 finishes quickly with a SAT result. 

repro: see the attached input file (and remove the .txt extension)
 
[infinite_loop.smt2.txt](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/1733079/infinite_loop.smt2.txt)

I'm using Haskell to create some Z3 formulas. When I try to run the following code I get segmentation fault (although other examples made by me work great). Does anybody know if that is a bug in Z3? The API used in Haskell is a mirror of the C API names:

```Haskell
import Z3.Monad
import Control.Monad.IO.Class
import Control.Applicative
import Data.Traversable
import Prelude hiding (sequence)

main = do
    out <- evalZ3 $ do
        is  <- mkIntSort
        _3  <- mkInteger 3
        _5  <- mkInteger 5
        _10 <- mkInteger 10
        x   <- mkFreshIntVar "x"
        y   <- mkFreshIntVar "y"
        y2  <- mkBvmul y y

        assert =<< mkAnd =<< mkEq x y2
   
    print "end"
```

Result : segmentation fault. 

It is caused by the line makes x y2.

> As a JVM developer willing to use non-java JVM languages (eg kotlin, scala, clojure, ...) I want to use Z3 with my application in a more fluent and less error prone fashion than mangled method calls, so that I can more quickly build my application and experience less complex-expression-fatigue related bugs.

The java API is not perfect. It exposes a large bulk of the functionality of Z3, but it does so in a way which conforms to many java idioms, which are not idioms 

My suggestion:

Using only the existing types we can write a set of kotlin extension functions that would make writing static expressions in Z3 much easier.

The four below uses could be identical:

```lisp
(declare-const x Real)
(declare-const y Real)

(assert (> (+ x y) 5))
(assert (> x 1))
(assert (> y 1))

(check-sat)
(get-model)
```

```py
x = Real('x')
y = Real('y')
s = Solver()

s.add(x + y > 5, x > 1, y > 1)

print(s.check())
print(s.model())
```

```java
Context ctx = new Context()

val x = ctx.mkRealConst("x")
val y = ctx.mkRealConst("y")
Solver s = ctx.mkSolver()

s.add(ctx.mkGt(ctx.mkAdd(x, y), ctx.mkReal("5")))
s.add(ctx.mkGt(x, ctx.mkInt(1))
s.add(ctx.mkGt(y, ctx.mkInt(1))

System.out.println(s.check())
System.out.println(s.getModel())
```

```kt
with(Context()){
  x = Real("x")
  y = Real("y")
  s = Solver()

  s.add(x + y gt 5, x gt 1, y gt 1)

  println(s.check())
  println(s.model())
}
```

---

as an aside: Every time I log bugs on libs (such as #1327) I feel guilty because I'm asking a group which owes me nothing to do something, albeit something that will likely benefit everybody. As I learn more about Z3 maybe ill eventually be able to pickup issues like that.

But I'm pretty knowledgeable about kotlin, so maybe I can offer you guys this feature instead.

issue_title: str.contains with symbolic string as first argument failing beyond equality
issue_body:
(I know string support is preliminary, so maybe this isn't completely implemented yet.)

When I use a symbolic string as the first argument in str.contains, the only string I can get back is one that's equal to the second argument. If I constrain this not to be true, then I get back the string with !0! at the beginning and !1! at the end. Here is an example:

```
(declare-const c0 String)
(define-fun e1 () Bool (= "barbar" c0))
(define-fun e2 () Bool (not e1))
(define-fun e3 () Bool (str.contains c0 "barbar"))
(assert e2)
(assert e3)
(check-sat)
(get-model)
```

`sat (model (define-fun c0 () String "!0!barbar!1!") )`

If I then constrain against that, assuming it is just the string that Z3 happened the generate, then it does not generate a distinct string, but instead generates the same string again. So I assume there is something happening on the back-end. Here is an example of that:

```
(declare-const c0 String)
(define-fun e1 () Bool (= "barbar" c0))
(define-fun e2 () Bool (not e1))
(define-fun e3 () Bool (str.contains c0 "barbar"))
(define-fun e4 () Bool (not (= "!0!barbar!1!" c0)))
(assert e2)
(assert e3)
(assert e4)
(check-sat)
(get-model)
```

`sat (model (define-fun c0 () String "!0!barbar!1!") )`

issue_title: Build fails on certain ARMv7 linux environments demanding -fPIC
issue_body:
On some but not all of my ARMv7 linux machines, linking fails with:

```
/usr/bin/ld: api/dll/mem_initializer.o: relocation R_ARM_THM_MOVW_ABS_NC against `a local symbol' can not be used when making a shared object; recompile with -fPIC
api/dll/mem_initializer.o: error adding symbols: Bad value
```

I get this failure on an Nvidia Jetson-TK1 devkit running their provided Ubuntu 14.04 and g++ 4.8.4 and on a https://www.scaleway.com/ server running Ubuntu 15.10 and g++ 5.2.1. It builds properly on a Raspberry Pi running Raspbian 8.0 (jessie) and g++ 4.9.2.

I have a patch that fixes it by adding -fPIC when building on armv7 but I want to think if there is a more fine-grained solution here, since it doesn't affect all my machines.

I'd like propose that publicly visible continuous integration (CI) testing is added by using [TravisCI](https://travis-ci.org/). This **does not** replace the internal testing already done. Instead its purpose is to provide early feedback by checking Z3 (and its bindings) will compile and can be installed.

The neat things about TravisCI is that it can be set up to build every pull request in addition to the current `master` branch. This is very handy because you can quickly see if outside contributions break the build. It will also send e-mails to the authors of commits that break the build.

TravisCI runs on Ubuntu Linux so this CI would only be for this platform. It is a free service for open source projects.

If this idea was accepted I would like to test the following configurations
- CXX=clang-3.7 Release build
- CXX=gcc-4.8 Release build
- CXX=clang-3.7 Debug build
- CXX=gcc-4.8 Debug build

We could test more configurations (e.g. different python versions) but the more configurations we add the longer it will take to complete the builds.

Each configuration would build Z3 and all the language bindings and check that install works correctly. Lightweight testing code be done on the Z3 binary in the future if desired.

I have quite a bit of experience setting up TravisCI so I'm happy to do the work here but I won't start unless I get the go-ahead from @NikolajBjorner and @wintersteiger because it would be a waste of my time to implement something that will never be accepted.

issue_title: Don't focus on editor when cell clicked
issue_body: Currently when you click in the output area it focuses the cursor in the editor. This is great for the sake of focusing the cell. It's not so great when you want to select outputs (especially if the overall output is fairly large).

From the [CodeMirror docs](https://codemirror.net/doc/manual.html):

> inputStyle: string
> Selects the way CodeMirror handles input and focus. The core library defines the "textarea" and "contenteditable" input models. On mobile browsers, the default is "contenteditable". On desktop browsers, the default is "textarea". Support for IME and screen readers is better in the "contenteditable" model. The intention is to make it the default on modern desktop browsers in the future.

Since we're in Chrome, we could likely change our default to contenteditable.

issue_title: SimProcedures do not return correctly if invoked with a jump instruction
issue_body:
If a SimProcedure is invoked with a jump instruction (for example, as a result of a tail call optimization), for example:
```asm
jmpq   489820 <__libc_realloc>
```
the angr simulation immediately terminates after running the SimProcedure instead of correctly continuing the program at the return address on the stack frame when the jump was invoked.

issue_title: _read_tle fails to handle bstar values that start with + character
issue_body:
_read_tle fails to handle bstar and mean_motion_sec_derivative values that start with + character.

Sometimes, these values starts with a '+'.

Example (from ISS):
1 25544U 98067A   15235.81765006 +.00009573 +00000-0 +14486-3 0  9999
2 25544 051.6452 106.3529 0001648 090.4174 004.9123 15.55401685958567

The + character is a valid character in this position, as indicated [by Celestrak](https://celestrak.com/columns/v04n03/:)
    
    "Columns with a '+' can have either a plus sign, a minus sign, or a space and columns with a '-' can have either a plus or minus sign (if the rest of the field is not blank)."

issue_title: Make how Root is setup for mergers more configurable
issue_body:
At present, GangaLHCb uses
```
[ROOT]
path = /cvmfs/lhcb.cern.ch/lib/RootConfig/pro/x86_64-slc6-gcc49-opt
```

And then sets ROOTSYS to this, LD_LIBRARY_PATH to this+/lib and then runs this+/bin/hadd. This fails badly if LHCb users are just running ganga straight (i.e. outside of lb-run) as the compiler (and other things) are not added to the LD_LIBRARY_PATH. This could be completely fixed by doing:
```
lb-run ROOT hadd
```
Instead but there is no config option to completely change the `rootprefix` used in the `RootMerger:mergefiles` function. I would propose adding this as a config option and setting the default LHCb setting to the above.

issue_title: Standardizing Web Archive Collection Directory Structure
issue_body:
Currently, pywb has the following dir structure, per collection (from http://pywb.readthedocs.io/en/latest/manual/configuring.html#directory-structure)

```
 <coll name>
        |
        +-- archives
        |     |
        |     +-- (WARC or ARC files here)
        |
        +-- indexes
        |     |
        |     +-- (CDXJ index files here)
        |
        +-- templates
        |     |
        |     +-- (optional html templates here)
        |
        +-- static
              |
              +-- (optional custom static assets here)
```

However, it would be great to have a standardized directory format that is not pywb specific that pywb could recognize. In addition to warcs and cdx, the dir structure could support other derivatives added by other tools, such as WAT files, extracted text. One possible idea is something like this:

```
 collection/
   - archives/  
   - indexes/
   - extractions/   (for WAT, full-text extraction)
   - replay_rules/  (for anything replay specific, such as static files, ui templates, etc..)
   - entrypoints/  (metadata about key pages, lists of urls to start browsing)

```

Of course, a lot of these may be product specific, especially the extractions and replay rules, but perhaps there is a way to standardize these?

The benefits of standardization will allow for easier transfer of collections across different service providers, institutions and repositories. It will also make it easier to store web archives in decentralized storage systems, such as DAT.

Any thoughts on the above convention, or other suggestions? All feedback welcome!

issue_title: Question: errors from queries involving pg_catalog tables running while things are being dropped
issue_body:
Hello! The impetus for the investigation that led to issue #101 is a class of errors that we've been seeing that seem to be due to concurrent access to pg_catalog tables. We have a test which makes sure that our alembic migrations are up to date. This test necessarily does a lot of reflection. We've been seeing this test fail intermittently with errors like these:

1
```
E       sqlalchemy.exc.InternalError: (psycopg2.InternalError) could not find tuple for constraint 839833
E        [SQL: '
        SELECT
          n.nspname as "schema",
          c.relname as "table_name",
          t.contype,
          t.conname,
          t.conkey,
          a.attnum,
          a.attname,
          pg_catalog.pg_get_constraintdef(t.oid, true) as condef,
          n.oid as "schema_oid",
          c.oid as "rel_oid"
        FROM pg_catalog.pg_class c
        LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_namespace n
          ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
        JOIN pg_catalog.pg_constraint t
          ON t.conrelid = c.oid
        JOIN pg_catalog.pg_attribute a
          ON t.conrelid = a.attrelid AND a.attnum = ANY(t.conkey)
        WHERE n.nspname !~ '^pg_'
        ORDER BY n.nspname, c.relname
        ']
```

2
```
E       sqlalchemy.exc.InternalError: (psycopg2.InternalError) cache lookup failed for relation 870981
E        [SQL: '
            SELECT
              n.nspname as "schema",
              c.relname as "table_name",
              d.column as "name",
              encoding as "encode",
              type, distkey, sortkey, "notnull", adsrc, attnum,
              pg_catalog.format_type(att.atttypid, att.atttypmod),
              pg_catalog.pg_get_expr(ad.adbin, ad.adrelid) AS DEFAULT,
              n.oid as "schema_oid",
              c.oid as "table_oid"
            FROM pg_catalog.pg_class c
            LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_namespace n
              ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
            JOIN pg_catalog.pg_table_def d
              ON (d.schemaname, d.tablename) = (n.nspname, c.relname)
            JOIN pg_catalog.pg_attribute att
              ON (att.attrelid, att.attname) = (c.oid, d.column)
            LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_attrdef ad
              ON (att.attrelid, att.attnum) = (ad.adrelid, ad.adnum)
            WHERE n.nspname !~ '^pg_'
            ORDER BY n.nspname, c.relname, att.attnum
            ']
```

3
```
E       sqlalchemy.exc.InternalError: (psycopg2.InternalError) cache lookup failed for relation 731237
E        [SQL: "
            SELECT c.oid
            FROM pg_catalog.pg_class c
            LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
            WHERE (pg_catalog.pg_table_is_visible(c.oid))
            AND c.relname = %(table_name)s AND c.relkind in ('r', 'v', 'm', 'f')
        "] [parameters: {'table_name': 'ref_cm_icd9'}]
```

We think that these errors happen when one of the other schemas in our database is being dropped while one of these queries is being run. Since these queries grab information about the entire database, they are not scoped to schemas at all. We have also found this page in the redshift docs: http://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/dg/c_serial_isolation.html which seems to be saying that this might be expected behavior.

Error number 1 is the one that we've seen the most. We think this is because of issue #101 - because the constraint query was being run many times, there was more of a chance that it would be run at the wrong time. However, we just recently saw error number 2, which is the query that this dialect uses for getting column info. Error number 3 actually a query from the regular postgres dialect, so this issue isn't specific to this dialect. We think that these sorts of errors happen specifically when functions are involved - some data is fetched, a function is run on some fetched id, and if the entity associated with that id disappears between those two steps, an error occurs.

I'm creating this issue more to ask if you guys have encountered something like this before and whether you've found any resolution. However, it may be useful for our purposes to restrict the `_get_all_*` queries so that they only query information about the requested schema. I don't know whether that would be a good change to make in upstream as well.

issue_title: Constraint information is not being cached
issue_body: In `RedshiftDialect`, there are three methods, `_get_all_relation_info`, `_get_all_column_info`, and `_get_all_constraint_info`, which query information about the whole cluster. These methods are called by several other methods which pass their `info_cache` argument down to one of the above methods. These methods, in turn, are called by other methods which, in most cases, pass down all of their keyword arguments. The one exception is `_get_redshift_constraints` - this method is never called with the keyword arguments of its caller, and therefore never gets the `info_cache` object that was passed in by SQLAlchemy. The result of this is that constraint information is never cached. When running something like alembic which does a lot of reflection, this can slow things down considerably because the dialect winds up querying information about the entire database every time alembic asks for anything.

I think that it would be beneficial to be able to pass a custom executor to a Graphene schema, so that it can use it to `execute` the query.


I'm thinking either:
```python
schema = Schema(query=QueryRootType, executor=Executor(...))
```
OR
```python
schema = Schema(query=QueryRootType)
schema.set_executor(Executor(...))
```

By default it should use the default executor instance. I am going to expose an API for this in `core.graphql.execution`

```python
from core.graphql import execution
default_executor = execution.get_default_executor()
```

The default executor will be configured to be a synchronous executor. In addition, you will be able to set the default executor using:

```python
execution.set_default_executor(Executor(...))
```

issue_title: Spinel: confusing overload of Interface Identifier term
issue_body:
The choice of the name for the "Interface Identifier" (IID) field in the Spinel header could lead to confusion.

_IP Version 6 Addressing Architecture_ [RFC 4291] defines that term, "Interface Identifier" (also abbreviated IID), to signify the 64-bit remainder of a global scope unicast address after the subnet prefix.

In IPv6 terms, this field in the Spinel header is more closely related to the "zone index" concept described in Section 6 _Zone Indices of IPv6 Scoped Address Architecture_ [[RFC 4007](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4007)], except its scope is limited to the communication channel between an OS its Spinel couterpart NCP.

I wonder if renaming this field as Zone Index Number (ZIN) would be helpful in avoiding this confusion.

issue_title: Cannot save the environment if it contains slashes
issue_body:
Hi, thank you for the great software first!

I am really enjoying using the software, however I found some trivial bugs that occur in corner cases.
When I try to save an environment which contains slashes, for example "model_1/dim100", it cannot save the environment and makes some unhappy side effects.
If I save it programmatically, for example `vis.save([env_name])`, the server makes error logs of `FileNotFoundError`, since the directory `$HOME/.visdom/model_1` does not exist.
And if I try to save it on the webpage, the webpage goes offline and I have to click the "offline" button or refresh the page to revive functionalities.

I think two solutions are possible:
1) making the directory (using something like `mkdir -p` or `os.makedirs`) before making the JSON file of an environment, or
2) replacing slash characters with other safe characters, like `_`.

issue_title: Curious issue with the FastText python API
issue_body:
I am currently working on a sentiment analysis project, I heard about the FastText classifier and wanted to see how well it performs on my dataset.

So i went through the code proposed on https://pypi.python.org/pypi/fasttext :

Let's say I have my text data and labels stored in 'data', a pandas dataframe
Now i split it into some train/test sets and write them into two .txt files

Each element of my dataset corresponds to one line of the .txt file, with the label as '__label__pos' or '__label__neg' (like in the examples from the fastText documentation)

`from sklearn.cross_validation import train_test_split`
`x_train, x_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(np.asarray(data['comment']), 
                                                 np.asarray(data['target']), test_size=0.2)`

`with open('train__Data.txt', 'a') as f:`
`     for i,X in enumerate(x_train):`
`        f.write(y_train[i] + ' ' + X)`
`        f.write('\n')`

`with open('test__Data.txt', 'a') as f:`
`     for i,X in enumerate(x_test):`
`        f.write(y_test[i] + ' ' + X)`
`        f.write('\n')`

So now, I should have everything prepared to run the training session.
`import fastext
classifier = fasttext.supervised('train__Data.txt', 'model')`

That piece of code actually does run and takes a little bit of time. It also successfully creates the model.bin file (it does'nt create model.vec, but i've read that was normal since they removed if for the supervised mode)

Now I should be ready to test it and/or get some predictions. Let's first run :
`result = classifier.test('test__Data.txt')
print('P@1:', result.precision)
print('R@1:', result.recall)
print('Number of examples:', result.nexamples)`

Which gives me the following output:

`P@1: nan
R@1: nan
Number of examples: 0`

My .txt files are well encoded as 'utf8'. I tried to experiment fastText with some other goals, like getting some embeddings from a train file and the same thing happened, the code seems to run just fine, takes a little bit of time, but then it can not output any vector representation.

train__Data.txt and test__Data.txt are about 70 and 20 Ko, the model.bin output about 800 Ko. So something does happen, I just can't figure how to test it/print some results!

I've went through the reported issues but couldn't find a similar case.

Am i missing something ?

issue_title: Fix help message for edit command
issue_body: The edit command no longer allows you to edit previous commands, but the help message says you can.
The py command needs a finally block which guarantees the "self._in_py" state variable gets reset to False in the end, even if an exception occurred.

Is there a best method to try first?  I'd guess oversample, but no idea which method. Any qualify as "not a bad first choice, of course your data matters and each have strength and weaknesses, but might as well start with...."

**First of all thank you for this great package!**

From what I recon G-mean can be easily used as a multi-class evaluation measure - it is simply the geometric mean of class-wise recalls. Therefore, for binary problems:
`G-mean = sqrt(TP/P * TN/N)`
or using a different notation
`G-mean = (class1_correct/class1 * class2_correct/class2)**(1/2.)`

For 3-class problems:
`G-mean = (class1_correct/class1 * class2_correct/class2,  * class3_correct/class3)**(1/3.)`

and so on changing the root/power to 1/4., 1/5., ...

There is no need for micro or macro recalls, in fact they diminish the value of G-mean, which is innately a multi-class measure (rare thing in imbalanced learning).

Sidenote:
One thing you probably know is that G-mean can very easily turn to zero - if one class is completely unrecognized the entire evaluation comes down to zero. With problems involving hundreds of classess thats not a nice thing. Therefore, sometimes people make a "correction" and one class is completely unrecognized instead of multiplying by zero they multiply by some (parmaterized) small value, e.g. 0.001.

issue_title: Implementation of the ays filesystem service
issue_body: See spec in #11

Postmark has introduced what they're calling "[modular webhooks](https://postmarkapp.com/blog/feature-announcement-modular-webhooks)." Anymail's existing Postmark webhooks work just fine with the changes, but the docs should be updated.

From what I can see, the Postmark changes that could affect Anymail are:
1. Postmark's webhook settings now has checkboxes to select which events you want to receive, rather than making you enter multiple URLs for the different event types. (Also, you can configure "bounce" separately from "spam complaint".)
2. There's a new RecordType field in the webhook json payload, to identify the event type. (But the rest of the payload is unchanged, and no fields have been removed.)

The first change only affects Anymail's docs on setting up Postmark webhooks.

The second change could be used to simplify Anymail's code that figures out event type from the payload. (But the fields that code uses now are still there, and haven't changed, so it's not urgent to update.)

issue_title: What Django versions should Anymail support?
issue_body:
Right now, Anymail is only building on Django 1.8 and 1.9.

Most of the code (from Djrill) is still compatible all the way back to Django 1.4. There are a few tests that are using some newer timezone pieces.

If it's important for people migrating from Djrill, it wouldn't be too hard to maintain compatibility with older Django versions.

But if everyone's already on Django 1.8 or later, that effort could probably be better spent elsewhere.

I'm planning to stop testing Python 3.3 after the next Anymail release (v2.1).

The only version of Django that supported Python 3.3 was Django 1.8, which [ended extended support](https://www.djangoproject.com/download/) on April 1, 2018.

I'm not immediately planning to drop Anymail support/testing for Django 1.8 on other Python versions, but Python 3.3 has a particularly problematic early release of the upgraded Python 3 email package. I can't imagine many people are using this combination, and tracking down oddities seems like a poor use of time. If you are using Django 1.8 on Python 3.3 with Anymail (or really anything having to do with email), you should upgrade to Python 3.4 or 3.5. Or downgrade to Python 2.7.

(I'll leave this issue open for feedback until Anymail v2.1 is ready to release, in case I've underestimated the impact.)

issue_body:
When installing a Python module library which used pybind11 I got an error message similar to [this stackoverflow question][q] because the include paths become different after installing the binary.

As a header-only library I think if it would be better to either

1. mark the headers as private instead of public, or
1. change the value based on if it is used build-time or install-time:

Quoting the quote from [the SO answer][a]:
> Include directories usage requirements commonly differ between the build-tree and the install-tree. The BUILD_INTERFACE and INSTALL_INTERFACE generator expressions can be used to describe separate usage requirements based on the usage location. Relative paths are allowed within the INSTALL_INTERFACE expression and are interpreted relative to the installation prefix. For example:
>
>     target_include_directories(mylib PUBLIC
>       $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include/mylib>
>       $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:include/mylib>  # <prefix>/include/mylib
>     ) 

[q]: http://stackoverflow.com/q/25676277/245614
[a]: http://stackoverflow.com/a/25681179/245614

issue_title: port number in URL is not respected
issue_body:
vdirsyncer 0.16.0 on Fedora 27

I sync successfully with one CardDAV server on a standard port.

A second server with a different name/IP is at the non-standard port 444, I have `url ="https://servername.tld:444/owncloudpath"` in the config. But discover errors out with `error: Unknown error occured: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='servername.tld', port=443)`, and the fingerprint also tells me that vdirsyncer indeed connects at port 443, not 444. Turning off verification completely does not help either. I gutess the port in the URL is simply stripped.

It might be useful to allow the script to detect if `scl enable httpd24 /bin/bash` is applied when searching for DocRoot.  As it stands, the script doesn't allow for installation when the default apache package is `httpd24-httpd`, rather than `httpd` on RHEL7/CentOS7.  It looks for vhosts in `/etc/httpd/conf.d/*` for `ServerName` and/or `ServerAlias`.  However, when httpd24-httpd from SoftwareCollections is used, those are specified in `/opt/rh/httpd24/root/etc/httpd/conf.d`.

issue_title: Auth: Add support for multiple auth mechanisms
issue_body:
Currently we're only supporting SOAP-based auth. It would be handy to support both SOAP and OAuth 2.0 (at least). Rough thoughts on how to implement this:

Create an `Authenticator` base class that each implementation (`soap.Authenticator`, `oauth.PasswordAuthenticator`, `oauth.AuthorizationAuthenticator`, etc) can inherit from. Implement a common `Tokens` structure that contains access and refresh tokens. Implement `authenticate` and `authorize` on the authenticators as required. Have them return the populated `Tokens` struct. Access token can be used in requests, refresh token can be used to auto-refresh access token as needed.

issue_title: When nodes become unreachable update the status in the graph
issue_body: Nodes may become unreachable. There is treatment for messages failing to be delivered but the graph still has information about those nodes which means when running something like `get_best_routes` it may return invalid paths. When finding nodes that are unreachable the graph should be updated to signal the condition and not try those paths.

Because of https://bugs.python.org/issue29051, a temporary solution (See https://github.com/gristlabs/asttokens/issues/4) was placed to avoid issues.

Current `sos status --html` is used by Jupyter `%taskinfo` to display task information. It would be nice to expand this option to display information directly in browser, especially for multiple tasks. This however goes to the task console business and we should perhaps design a dedicated interface for it.

That will be a lot of JS and we should wrap it as a dedicated project similar to what rq and celery are doing.

AttributeError: 'Page' object has no attribute 'id'
```

Printing `dir(page)` shows that while `page` indeed has no `id` attribute, it does have `course_id`, `page_id`, and `parent_id` attributes. Manually setting `page.id = page.page_id` before the `Page.edit` call does seem to fix the problem, though of course that is only a workaround.

We have several endpoints that are prefixed with `list_` while others are prefixed with `get_`. For consistency's sake, we need to standardize which form to use. After internal discussion, we have decided that `get_` seems the most appropriate.

Change all method names that start with `list_` to start with `get_`.

Because some folks may already be using the `list_` methods, we can't just rename the methods - we'll need deprecate them and direct users to switch to the new `get_` methods. Most likely move the code to the `get_` version and have the `list_` version warn with a `DeprecationWarning` and call the `get_` version.

issue_title: find(name) on element-kind of resource classes raises NotFound for existing resource
issue_labels: [{'id': 429586299, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/area:%20code', 'name': 'area: code', 'color': '207de5', 'default': False}, {'id': 429588767, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/resolution:%20fixed', 'name': 'resolution: fixed', 'color': 'cccccc', 'default': False}, {'id': 429587708, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': '84b6eb', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
### Actual behavior

`nics.find(name)` raises `NotFound` for an existing NIC resource.

This is caused by the fact that the `list()` method for NICs and other element-kind of resources returns Python resource objects that do not have a name property, so the operation that is supposed to populate the name-URI-cache does not add anything. Because of the optimization in find(name) to always use the name-URI-cache, it relies on that cache being populated.

This issue applies to all element-kind of resource types (NIC, HBA, VF, Port).

### Expected behavior

Resource should be found.

### Execution environment

* zhmcclient version: 0.12.1.dev19
* Operating system (type+version): RHEL 7.3
* HMC version: 2.13.1
* CPC version: 2.13.1

In utils.py there a method:
```
def is_url(value):
    return value and \
           (value.startswith('http://') or
            value.startswith('https://') or
            value.startswith('/'))
```

What if somebody would like to redirect their users directly to his app, like: `my_app://success_login?token=...`
I think you don't need to verify URI at all, unless `SOCIAL_AUTH_SANITIZE_REDIRECTS` was not specified. The client resource always knows what the result he should expect from his `next` argument.

Previewing update of stack 'count-api-testing'

issue_title: Currently, the "text that should start with tab" and the "general sentence" are executed as .txt file without any distinction.
issue_body: Is there any way to separate sentences that begin with tabs in the ".txt" file with "space"?

**Readme:**
- I am having a PDF attached below, is it possible to get each paragraph as one string  
[drylab.pdf](https://github.com/pdfminer/pdfminer.six/files/2058131/drylab.pdf)

EX: 
```
Welcome to our first newsletter of 2017! It's been a while since the last one, and a lot has
happened. We promise to keep them coming every two months hereafter, and permit
ourselves to make this one rather long. The big news is the beginnings of our launch in
the American market, but there are also interesting updates on sales, development,
mentors and (of course) the investment round that closed in January.
```

When writing a FixedString column, the data is first encoded using utf-8 to bytes in Python. This could cause a problem when the string cannot be encoded using utf-8. For instance, cipher text bytes generated by hmac sha256. I would suggest add type checking before encoding in https://github.com/mymarilyn/clickhouse-driver/blob/master/src/writer.py#L18 and https://github.com/mymarilyn/clickhouse-driver/blob/master/src/writer.py#L24. If the text is already bytes, just don't encode it.

issue_title: Tickets table: dynamic table sorting overrides query sort order?
issue_body: Forgive me if is obvious, but I searched the other tickets carefully and did not see this reported. It looks like the new dynamic sorting on the Tickets table (provided by dataTables?) overrides the sort order specified by the Ticket Query. The Tickets are always sorted by ascending pk, regardless of the sort order filter. Here's a screenshot -- you can see the sort order is creation date, but the table is actually sorted by pk. As far as I can tell, this always happens. I noticed you can see this bug in the screenshots shared last year when this feature was first released: https://github.com/django-helpdesk/django-helpdesk/pull/419#issuecomment-246574490 I'm happy with the dynamic sorting - the new UI is fabulous, thank-you! But did find this confusing.

**What happens;** ``` num_to_link('this is ticket#999') u"this is ticke #999" ``` **What I expected to happen** ``` num_to_link('this is ticket#999') u"this is ticket #999" ``` **Why:** `regex = r"(?:[^&]|\b|^)#(\d+)\b"` So ... (non-capturing) match a character that is: not "&" OR is a word boundary OR is the start of the line Then, find a "#", then capture digits until you hit a word boundary Have I read this right? If so, what is the purpose of the non-capturing 'not a "&" ' part? It swallows the last character before the #, but was it meant to not capture a string like "ϧ" ?

issue_title: continuous_update on IntText requires mouseout before updating
issue_body:
I was having difficulty figuring out why widgets were slow to interact with a changing IntText when modified by the up and down arrows. With continuous_update set to False (default), the IntText actually requires a mouseout (i.e. move the cursor off the widget button) before the observed function occurs.

Is this intended behaviour? I would have expected the function to fire once I had stopped depressing the mouse key if I wanted to change the IntText by a large amount at once e.g. increase from 10 to 30, and see the reaction as soon as I stop holding the mouse key when the text hits 30.

I first suggested this change in #108.

I would split `SentinelAPIError` into a couple of more specific ones:

  - `SentinelAPIError`: for relaying API responses that return an exception, e.g. for invalid query parameters. The response is expected to contain the `cause-message` in that case (as in #107).
  - `UnauthorizedError`: for cases where invalid credentials were provided or when the server does not accept them yet for whatever reason (a common issue for new users, I think).
  - `ServerError`: for cases when the server is unreachable, under maintenance or the response is just unparsable garbage.

This would enable different error cases to be caught more elegantly. E.g. you probably just want to retry your query after some delay in case of `ServerError`.

issue_title: When using fully qualified file locations with minio the domain endpoint is missing
issue_body: The endpoint for S3 requests is currently a default and need to be an explicit valid net endpoint for tools such as minio and upspin to work.

Add the option to add custom command line options to linters. For example
```
[command_line:multiline_import]
flake8.ignore += E401
```
If one would now use ``snekchek --multiline_import`` he would run his normal config BUT ignore the flake8 error E401. I think the best way to achieve this would be to use .snekrc as a template for the real config file which could for example be named .snekcache. We could use [this](https://docs.python.org/3.4/library/argparse.html#partial-parsing) for handling that with argparse

Maps, Sets and Lists wrap the native types - to return the instance back on operations that do not have a return value by default.

The current mechanism does a shallow copy of these objects, which is inefficient.  Instead of extending the base objects, the complex types should contain the object which will save on shallow copies.

issue_title: Add a helper wrapper for constructing tensor initializers
issue_body:
Simple task - add helper utilities for constructing and manipulating initializers to https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/onnx/helper.py

Example code doing something similar: `make_tensor` in https://github.com/onnx/onnxmltools/blob/master/onnxmltools/proto/helper.py#L77

We've been keeping an eye on annoyances when we adjust PyTorch for latest changes in the ONNX proto, and we noticed some for https://github.com/onnx/onnx/pull/51

* Not all protobuf messages have the `Proto` suffix anymore; in particular, the nested protobuf `Dimension` doesn't have the suffix. This makes it look strange when compared with all the other protobuf messages. Can we make this consistent?

* Additionally, `TypeProto` defines nested protobufs. I wasn't sure how this was going to look in tooling, but we have discovered what happens with nanopb: you now get extremely ugly protobuf names like `TypeProto_TensorTypeProto`. This is especially ugly because `Type` is redundantly specified twice. Can we just put it at the top level of the protobuf?

issue_title: Tornado >=4.5 support still broken in some cases
issue_body: auto tornado assumes "handler_class" and "regex" properties on the "spec" objects, consistent with the URLSpec object. However, in Tornado 4.5, routes may be rules (and URLSpec is a subclass of rule), and rules have "target" and "matcher" properties (with regex @ matcher.regex).

issue_title: Matlab-parser fails while parsing new type extensions of the properties
issue_body:
Dear all,

In the last 3 Years Mathworks made a lot of changes and extensions for the Matlab-language, i.e. in OO-programming.

Currently I'm documenting my Matlab-Library for my thesis. In this library I needed to use very often the __*new (undocumented) syntax extensions*__ to enforce the __*property types*__ for the *class property names*. These new extensions are very helpful and makes the verification very easy if a property has the correct type. See following discussions below:
- [https://undocumentedmatlab.com/blog/setting-class-property-types](https://undocumentedmatlab.com/blog/setting-class-property-types)
- [https://stackoverflow.com/questions/7192048/can-i-assign-types-to-class-properties-in-matlab/15992558#15992558](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/7192048/can-i-assign-types-to-class-properties-in-matlab/15992558#15992558)

In general, the new syntax for the class properties is defined as follows:
```
properties
   propName@className dimensionType = initialValue;
end
```
For the `dimensionType` there are three possible options, *matrix*, *vector* or *scalar*.

I have made a very simple test class which has only properties. For example this simple class works in Sphinx with your latest *matlabdomain* very well:
```
classdef MyTestDataType
    % MYTESTDATATYPE is a data type (class) for specific links.
    %
    % Args:
    %   link_name (string):    Name of the reference link.
    %   pos (double, vector):  Cartesian position of the link.
    %   rotm (double, matrix): (3 x 3) rotation matrix.
    %   idx (uint8, scalar):   Index of the reference link.
    properties
        link_name = 'none';
        pos       = zeros(3,1);
        rotm      = eye(3,3);
        idx       = 0;
    end
end
```

But when I replace the above properties with
```
link_name@char     = 'none';
pos@double  vector = zeros(3,1);
rotm@double matrix = zeros(3,3);
idx@uint8   scalar = 0;
```

    raise TypeError('Expected property.')

```

Sphinx is with its plugins one of the best documentation tools, but unfortunately all current parser for documenting code in Matlab doesn't support this new syntax.

Is it possible to extend the parser with this new syntax s.t. Sphinx can interpret the new property syntax like below (in Google style with Napoleon)?
```
% Args:
%   link_name (char):      Name of the reference link.
%   pos (double, vector):  Cartesian position of the link.
%   rotm (double, matrix): (3 x 3) rotation matrix.
%   idx (uint8, scalar):   Index of the reference link.
```

Or optional, which should be easier, is it possible that the new syntax right beside the property name will be ignored by the parser?

By the way, I have to set also a *comment header* into each file (copyright, license, etc.), like in C/C++:

%* This is an Comment Header
%  Copyright (C) 2018, by John Doe.
%
%  some descriptions ...
%
%  lisence (GPL, BSD, etc.)
%*%

How is it possible, or how can I force, that the parser of the matlabdomain will ignore this comment header without raising any errors?

Thank you very much in advance!

With best regards,
Ganimed.

Currently, I must specify the names of each layer, i.e, when I want to use two dense layer, I have to name them 'dense1' and 'dense2'.

But in tensorflow.contrib.layers, it is ok to have two dense layers without specify their names.

Can it be done in tensorlayer?

issue_title: toil-cwl-runner works on single and fails on batch, mesos, aws
issue_body:
If I launch a cluster like so:

```
toil launch-cluster test \
    --leaderNodeType t2.xlarge \
    --keyPairName mykey \
    --zone us-east-1a \
    -p aws
```

and set it up properly then run `toil-cwl-runner`:

```
PRIVATE_IP=$(curl http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/local-ipv4); 
toil-cwl-runner \
--maxNodes 2 \
--nodeTypes t2.xlarge \
--debugWorker \
--provisioner aws \
--batchSystem mesos \
--mesosMaster ${PRIVATE_IP}:5050 \
--disableCaching \
--defaultMemory 6G \
--defaultCores 4 \
--logDebug \
--logFile batchSystem.nonpreempt.err.log \
/tmp/workflow.cwl \
/tmp/input.json
```

I will get a bunch of failures at, e.g.:

```
$grep Totally batchSystem.nonpreempt.err.log
Totally failed job: 'file:///tmp/qc/fastqc.cwl' fastqc B/X/jobaqaKIn is marking direct predecessor: 'CWLScatter' w/x/jobo1VwN5 as having failed jobs
Totally failed job: 'file:///tmp/qc/fastqc.cwl' fastqc p/9/job5rt616 is marking direct predecessor: 'CWLScatter' w/x/jobo1VwN5 as having failed jobs
Totally failed job: 'CWLScatter' w/x/jobo1VwN5 is marking direct predecessor: 'CWLWorkflow' 0/g/job4OpGO1 as having failed jobs
Totally failed job: 'file:///tmp/utils/extract-basename.cwl' echo S/e/jobDayC9g is marking direct predecessor: 'CWLScatter' 7/p/jobnZCDj2 as having failed jobs
Totally failed job: 'file:///tmp/utils/extract-basename.cwl' echo r/W/jobcb_RM_ is marking direct predecessor: 'CWLScatter' 7/p/jobnZCDj2 as having failed jobs
Totally failed job: 'CWLScatter' 7/p/jobnZCDj2 is marking direct predecessor: 'CWLWorkflow' 0/g/job4OpGO1 as having failed jobs
Totally failed job: 'CWLWorkflow' 0/g/job4OpGO1 is marking direct predecessor: 'CWLWorkflow' D/x/joboPYr0i as having failed jobs
```

It works on single machine mode, e.g.:

```
PRIVATE_IP=$(curl http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/local-ipv4); 
toil-cwl-runner \
--defaultMemory 6G \
--defaultCores 4 \
--logDebug \
--logFile singleMachine.nonpreempt.err.log \
/tmp/workflow.cwl \
/tmp/input.json
```
...so I suspect it has something to do with mesos communicating between the leader and worker nodes (see a snippet of `/var/lib/mesos/mesos-master.INFO` below). I don't think it has to do with my version of toil; I've tested several. Any ideas?

```

观察了该touch动作的参数，发现record_pos这个参数是负数，原来这个参数的原点是当前屏幕的中心点，但是图像识别时的坐标系又是以屏幕左上角为原点计算的，不知道是不是这个差别导致了sift识别时会选取到屏幕外边的点，望解答。

issue_title: 0.3.3以后的K线图不显示提示的【open, close, lowest, highest】了
issue_body: 0.3.3以后的K线图不显示提示的【open, close, lowest, [highest】了]

Currently users have to decide at compile time if they would like to build a binary that supports AVX2 intrinsics or not. If they build with AVX2 intrinsics and end up deploying to somewhere that lacks AVX2 intrinsics, they will suffer a segfault due to the illegal instruction. Though users can build without AVX2 intrinsics and it will work fine regardless of whether the target infrastructure has AVX2 support, the compression algorithms here may run slower than if they were built with AVX2 support. Admittedly avoiding a segfault is much more important than degraded performance.

However, in the ideal case, we could build `numcodecs` with and without AVX2 support and then merely detect at runtime whether AVX2 instructions were permitted and thus choose the appropriate code path without crashing in either case. This will take a bit of work to understand where AVX2 instructions are being introduced and how to avoid them. Though some of that was already done in the first referenced issue below.

xref: https://github.com/zarr-developers/zarr/issues/136
xref: https://github.com/zarr-developers/numcodecs/issues/24
xref: https://github.com/zarr-developers/numcodecs/pull/26
xref: https://github.com/zarr-developers/numcodecs/pull/27

issue_title: How to use GLSL lark parser on a QScintilla
issue_body:
Hi, nice to meet you.

Today I've discovered this really promising project and although I haven't tested it yet from what I've read I can say it looks very promising to use. Few days ago I was testing this little pyparsing [glsl parser](https://stackoverflow.com/a/39705506/3809375) and the results I was getting weren't too great for simple piece of code (~0.2-0.3s) so I wanted to give it a shot to lark to see whether I'll be getting faster results.

That said, I've opened this [question](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/50459326/qscintilla-glsl-lexer) couple of days ago but since I've discovered lark I was thinking that instead considering using a `QsciLexerCPP` subclass to address this problem (that is just wrong) I'd write my own `QsciLexerCustom` subclass using lark, more info about [here](https://qscintilla.com/subclass-qscilexercustom). But some questions arise to me:

 - Assuming I've got the lark grammar and built the AST, how would you create the styles with it on a QSciScintilla, could we come up with a little example to illustrate this? Could we come up with a 5-min example showing how to use one of the lark examples on a qscintilla?
 - I'll want proper glsl syntax highlighting on the qscintilla editor but I'll also want to show autocompletions of function definitions, function declarations, ... , will be that possible to achieve by using lark?
 - Is there any freenode IRC channel / forum / SO tag to talk/discuss about lark? I'm posting questions here assuming you'll have the tag "help_wanted" :)

Got few more questions but I don't want to make the thread longer, summing up, trying to learn how to use lark to syntax highlight text on a QScintilla component properly.

Thanks in advance!
Bruno

I'm note sure whether it is a bug or not, but since version 0.6.0, the parsing of the following EBNF (extract, [here is the full file](https://github.com/rezemika/oh_sanitizer/blob/e2c3a0b80d7c50bad2a91b9d3f20517119359898/oh_sanitizer/field.ebnf#L105)) raises an AssertionError.

```
MONTH : "Jan"i | "Feb"i | "Mar"i | "Apr"i | "May"i | "Jun"i | "Jul"i | "Aug"i | "Sep"i | "Oct"i | "Nov"i | "Dec"i
year.2 : /([0-9]{4})+/

%import common.INT
PLUS_OR_MINUS : "+" | "-"
day_offset : " " PLUS_OR_MINUS INT (" day" ["s"] | " d")

monthday_date : [year " "] MONTH " " INT -> monthday_date_monthday
              | [year " "] MONTH " " INT "-" INT -> monthday_date_day_to_day
              | [year " "] MONTH -> monthday_date_month
              | [year " "] EASTER -> monthday_date_easter
              | [year " "] EASTER [day_offset] -> monthday_date_easter
```

Here is the traceback.

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "oh_sanitizer/main.py", line 22, in get_parser
    return lark.Lark(grammar, start="time_domain", parser="earley")
  File "/home/rezemika/Programmes/Python/oh_sanitizer/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/lark/lark.py", line 160, in __init__
    tokens, self.rules, self.ignore_tokens = self.grammar.compile()
  File "/home/rezemika/Programmes/Python/oh_sanitizer/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/lark/load_grammar.py", line 484, in compile
    expansions = rule_tree_to_text.transform(tree)
  File "/home/rezemika/Programmes/Python/oh_sanitizer/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/lark/visitors.py", line 53, in transform
    return self._transform_tree(tree)
  File "/home/rezemika/Programmes/Python/oh_sanitizer/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/lark/visitors.py", line 49, in _transform_tree
    children = list(self._transform_children(tree.children))
  File "/home/rezemika/Programmes/Python/oh_sanitizer/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/lark/visitors.py", line 44, in _transform_children
    yield self._transform_tree(c) if isinstance(c, Tree) else c
  File "/home/rezemika/Programmes/Python/oh_sanitizer/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/lark/visitors.py", line 50, in _transform_tree
    return self._call_userfunc(tree, children)
  File "/home/rezemika/Programmes/Python/oh_sanitizer/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/lark/visitors.py", line 39, in _call_userfunc
    return f(children)
  File "/home/rezemika/Programmes/Python/oh_sanitizer/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/lark/load_grammar.py", line 250, in alias
    assert _alias is None, (alias, expansion, '-', _alias)  # Double alias not allowed
AssertionError: (Token(RULE, 'monthday_date_easter'), ([NonTerminal('year'), Terminal('__IGNORE_0'), Terminal('EASTER'), Terminal('__IGNORE_0')], None), '-', ([NonTerminal('year'), Terminal('__IGNORE_0'), Terminal('EASTER'), Terminal('__IGNORE_0'), NonTerminal('day_offset')], None))
```

This error is solved with the following.

```
monthday_date : [year " "] MONTH " " INT -> monthday_date_monthday
              | [year " "] MONTH " " INT "-" INT -> monthday_date_day_to_day
              | [year " "] MONTH -> monthday_date_month
              | [year " "] EASTER -> monthday_date_easter
              | [year " "] EASTER day_offset -> monthday_date_easter
```

So, I'm note sure it's a bug or a feature, but just in case...

Thank you for this great module! :)

issue_title: Maybe look into better ways to optimize match_schemas
issue_body:
In https://github.com/tebeka/fastavro/pull/199 we check if the writer and reader schemas are the same and if so set the reader_schema to `None` so that we don't spend time in `match_schemas` which is actually quite costly:

https://github.com/tebeka/fastavro/blob/14f5833ff2e6d05a65ebfb8e5d69f9d5da511852/fastavro/_read_py.py#L71-L104

However, if the schemas are basically the same but the reader has a tiny difference, we won't set it to `None` and will again spend a lot of time in `match_schemas`. Therefore, it might be good to do some optimizing in `match_schemas`. Perhaps it helps to do an equality check there before do the normal avro schema evolution check?

issue_title: N3 serialization fails for certain Literals
issue_body:
bernhard...@gmail.com, 2011-08-23T07:08:34.000Z 
A Serialization/deserialization roundtrip of a certain class of Literals fails when there are both, newline characters and multiple subsequent quotation marks in the lexical form of the Literal (see below). In this case invalid N3 is emitted by the serializer, which in turn cannot be parsed correctly.

What steps will reproduce the problem?
>>> from rdflib.term import URIRef, Literal
>>> from rdflib.graph import ConjunctiveGraph
>>> g=ConjunctiveGraph()
>>> g.add((URIRef('http://foobar'), URIRef('http://fooprop'), Literal('abc\ndef"""""')))
>>> g.serialize(format='n3') # emits invalid N3
'@prefix ns1:  .\n\nns1:foobar ns1:fooprop """abc\ndef\\""""\\"""" .\n\n'
>>> g2=ConjunctiveGraph()
>>> g2.parse(data=g.serialize(format='n3'), format='n3')
------------------------------------------------------------
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "", line 1, in 
  File "/site-python/rdflib/graph.py", line 988, in parse
    location=location, file=file, data=data, **args)
  File "/site-python/rdflib/graph.py", line 784, in parse
    parser.parse(source, self, **args)
  File "/site-python/rdflib/plugins/parsers/notation3.py", line 2257, in parse
    p.loadStream(source.getByteStream())
  File "/site-python/rdflib/plugins/parsers/notation3.py", line 892, in loadStream
    return self.loadBuf(stream.read())   # Not ideal
  File "/site-python/rdflib/plugins/parsers/notation3.py", line 898, in loadBuf
    self.feed(buf)
  File "/site-python/rdflib/plugins/parsers/notation3.py", line 924, in feed
    i = self.directiveOrStatement(str,j)
  File "/site-python/rdflib/plugins/parsers/notation3.py", line 939, in directiveOrStatement
    if j>=0: return self.checkDot(str,j)
  File "/site-python/rdflib/plugins/parsers/notation3.py", line 1478, in checkDot
    str, j, "expected '.' or '}' or ']' at end of statement")
BadSyntax: at line 4 of <>:
Bad syntax (expected '.' or '}' or ']' at end of statement) at ^ in:
"...fix ns1:  .

ns1:foobar ns1:fooprop """abc
def\""""^\"""" .

"
>>> 

What is the expected output? What do you see instead?
In correct output all quotation marks in the Literal should be escaped, except the trailing three ones. This can be reliably parsed.
>>> data='@prefix ns1:  .\n\nns1:foobar ns1:fooprop """abc\ndef\\"\\"\\"\\"\\"""" .\n\n'
>>> g3=ConjunctiveGraph()
>>> g3.parse(data=data, format='n3')
)>
>>> g.isomorphic(g3)
True
>>> 


What version of the product are you using? On what operating system?
revision 37d3cbfff340

Please provide any additional information below.

Comment 1 by gromgull
I also came across this - it's even more broken with ntriples (which do not allow triple quoted strings) 

I will sit down and work the regex-fu some day...


Comment 2 by bernhard...@gmail.com
For fixing the NT output I hacked a workaround some time ago, but this relies on valid N3 output. Its for sure not the most elegant way to do it, but I didn't find yet a case where it does not work (except the cases where invalid N3 is emitted).


Comment 3 by gjhigg...@gmail.com
Is this actually a bug or just an infelicity of expression, allowed by Python's extra-flexible string delimiters? 

N3 strings are delimited by doublequote, singlequote does not need to be escaped - so the object of the statement in question might be more appropriately, if more cumbersomely, expressed and escaped as: 

Literal("abc\\ndef\"\"\"\"\"")

And then the round-tripping flows smoothly ...

>>> from rdflib.term import URIRef, Literal
>>> from rdflib.graph import ConjunctiveGraph
>>> g=ConjunctiveGraph()
>>> g.add((URIRef('http://foobar'), URIRef('http://fooprop'), Literal("abc\\ndef\"\"\"\"\"")))
>>> g.serialize(format='n3') # would appear to emit valid N3 this time
'@prefix ns1:  .\n\nns1:foobar ns1:fooprop "abc\\\\ndef\\"\\"\\"\\"\\"" .\n\n'
>>> g2=ConjunctiveGraph()
>>> g2.parse(data=g.serialize(format='n3'), format='n3')
)>
>>> g2.serialize(format="n3")
'@prefix ns1:  .\n\nns1:foobar ns1:fooprop "abc\\\\ndef\\"\\"\\"\\"\\"" .\n\n'
>>> print(g2.serialize(format="n3"))
@prefix ns1:  .

ns1:foobar ns1:fooprop "abc\\ndef\"\"\"\"\"" .


>>> g.isomorphic(g2)

Comment 1 by eik...@gmail.com



Comment 2 by eik...@gmail.com



Comment 3 by drewpca
A file with @prefix isn't in the 'nt' format, which is a very restricted line-based 
format for RDF. Your file has a confusing extension, and I confirm that the source 
page is being misleading by showing "N-Triples with @prefix".

I added a note about this in the rdflib parser class:

% pydoc rdflib.syntax.parsers.NTParser

    class NTParser(rdflib.syntax.parsers.Parser)
     |  parser for the ntriples format, often stored with the .nt extension
     |  
     |  See http://www.w3.org/TR/rdf-testcases/#ntriples
     |  

http://code.google.com/p/rdflib/source/detail?r=1735
